final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an Amazon Linux 2 EC2 Instance using the latest AMI.
  It includes a VPC with a public subnet, an Internet Gateway, routing, and a security group
  that allows SSH access. The instance type is configurable via a parameter.
Parameters:
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type
    Type: String
    Default: t2.micro

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicRT"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Enable SSH access via port 22
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SG"

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2}}"
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-Instance"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a DynamoDB table for storing ArtistId, Concert, and TicketSales.
  The table uses ArtistId as the Partition Key and Concert as the Sort Key.
  It uses PAY_PER_REQUEST billing mode, server-side encryption with the default AWS managed CMK,
  and Point-in-Time Recovery. Tags are applied for project and environment cost allocation.

Parameters:
  ProjectName:
    Type: String
    Default: "MyProject"
    Description: "Name of the project for tagging and resource naming."
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: "dev"
    Description: "Deployment environment (e.g., dev, prod) for tagging and resource naming."

Resources:
  ArtistConcertDynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-${Environment}-ArtistConcertTable"
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: ArtistId
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: Concert
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: ArtistId
          KeyType: HASH
        - AttributeName: Concert
          KeyType: RANGE
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
        PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

Description: >
  AWS CloudFormation Template that creates an EC2 instance with an associated Security Group.
Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access.
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Default: existing-keypair
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type.
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
  SSHLocation:
    Description: The IP address range that is allowed SSH access to the EC2 instance (e.g., 0.0.0.0/0).
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
  VpcId:
    Description: The VPC ID in which to launch the EC2 instance.
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Default: vpc-0abc1234
  SubnetId:
    Description: The Subnet ID within the selected VPC.
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Default: subnet-0abc1234

Mappings:
  AWSRegionToAMI:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c2b8ca1dad447f8a
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-032509848e8fcdeb6
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0bdb828fd58c52235
    eu-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0520e698dd500b1d1

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Enable SSH access on port 22
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref SSHLocation
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: !FindInMap
        - AWSRegionToAMI
        - !Ref "AWS::Region"
        - AMI
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup

Outputs:

    Description: The Instance ID of the newly created EC2 instance.
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  InstancePublicIP:
    Description: Public IP address of the newly created EC2 instance.
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy an EC2 instance (t3.micro) using
  a region-specific AMI with secure remote management enabled via AWS Systems Manager,
  and a minimal security group for outbound access.

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Description: >
      The VPC ID where the EC2 instance will be deployed.
      Update this default value to a valid VPC ID in your target environment if desired.
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Default: vpc-01234567

Mappings:
  RegionMap:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0123456789abcdef0
    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-0abcdef1234567890ab
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0fedcba9876543210
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-00112233445566778

Resources:
  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EC2InstanceRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-InstanceProfile"
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Minimal security group for EC2 instance with outbound access
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress: []
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      ImageId: !FindInMap [RegionMap, !Ref AWS::Region, AMI]
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EC2Instance"

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: ID of the created EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Template that creates an encrypted one-zone EFS file system in a configurable Availability Zone along with its VPC, subnet, and necessary security group.

Parameters:
  AvailabilityZone:
    Description: The Availability Zone where the subnet and mount target will be created.
    Type: String
    Default: us-east-1a

Resources:
  MyVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: MyVPC

  MySubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Ref AvailabilityZone
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "MySubnet-${AvailabilityZone}"

  EFSSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for EFS mount target to allow inbound NFS traffic.
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 2049
          ToPort: 2049
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EFSSecurityGroup

  MyEFS:
    Type: AWS::EFS::FileSystem
    Properties:
      Encrypted: true
      FileSystemTags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: MyEFS

  MyEFSMountTarget:
    Type: AWS::EFS::MountTarget
    Properties:
      FileSystemId: !Ref MyEFS
      SubnetId: !Ref MySubnet
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref EFSSecurityGroup

Outputs:
  EFSFileSystemId:
    Description: ID of the encrypted EFS file system.
    Value: !Ref MyEFS

  EFSMountTargetId:
    Description: ID of the EFS mount target.
    Value: !Ref MyEFSMountTarget

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  This CloudFormation template deploys an Amazon EC2 instance in a pre-existing default VPC and subnet.
  The EC2 instance uses the latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI retrieved via SSM Parameter Store, attaches
  ephemeral instance store volumes via block device mappings, and uses an existing SSH key pair for secure access.

Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: "Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance."
  
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: "The ID of the pre-existing default VPC."

  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: "The ID of the pre-existing default subnet within the default VPC."

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security group to allow SSH access."
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: m5d.large
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2:1}}"
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: 0
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/sdb
          VirtualName: ephemeral0
        - DeviceName: /dev/sdc
          VirtualName: ephemeral1

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: "The Instance ID of the newly created EC2 instance."
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  PublicIP:
    Description: "Public IP address of the EC2 instance."
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a DynamoDB table for mobile notifications reporting
  with a global secondary index on 'type' and 'sentTime', PAY_PER_REQUEST billing, and TTL enabled.

Parameters:
  TableNameSuffix:
    Type: String
    Description: Suffix to append to the DynamoDB table name for uniqueness.
    Default: notifications

Resources:
  MobileNotificationsReportingDDB:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub "mobile-notifications-reporting-${TableNameSuffix}-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: notificationId
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: type
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: sentTime
          AttributeType: N
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: notificationId
          KeyType: HASH
      GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: TypeSentTimeIndex
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: type
              KeyType: HASH
            - AttributeName: sentTime
              KeyType: RANGE
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL
      TimeToLiveSpecification:
        AttributeName: expiryTime
        Enabled: true

Outputs:
  DynamoDBTableName:
    Description: The name of the DynamoDB table for mobile notifications reporting.
    Value: !Ref MobileNotificationsReportingDDB

    Value: !GetAtt MobileNotificationsReportingDDB.Arn

    Type: String
    Default: "t2.micro"

Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a single Elastic Network Interface (ENI)
  with two secondary private IP addresses and two Elastic IPs (EIPs) that are
  associated with the secondary private IP addresses. This enables a single
  EC2 instance to handle multiple public-facing IP addresses via one ENI.

Parameters:
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: The subnet ID where the ENI will be created.

    Default: "{{resolve:ssm:/your-app/validSecurityGroupId:1}}"

      GroupSet:
        - !Ref SecurityGroupId
      SecondaryPrivateIpAddressCount: 2

      Domain: vpc

  EIP2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIP
    Properties:
      Domain: vpc

  EIPAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIPAssociation
    Properties:
      AllocationId: !GetAtt EIP1.AllocationId
      NetworkInterfaceId: !Ref MyENI
      PrivateIpAddress: !Select [0, !GetAtt MyENI.SecondaryPrivateIpAddresses]

  EIPAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIPAssociation
    Properties:
      AllocationId: !GetAtt EIP2.AllocationId
      NetworkInterfaceId: !Ref MyENI
      PrivateIpAddress: !Select [1, !GetAtt MyENI.SecondaryPrivateIpAddresses]

Outputs:

  EIP1:
    Description: The first allocated Elastic IP.

  EIP2:

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an API Gateway REST API with a non-proxy
  Lambda integration using AWS Lambda (python3.12) and API Gateway with proper
  integration mapping, deployment, and permissions.

Parameters:
  LambdaTimeout:
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
    Description: Timeout for the Lambda function in seconds.

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

  LambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: NonProxyIntegrationLambda
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: !Ref LambdaTimeout
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Log the event received
              print("Received event: " + json.dumps(event))
              return {
                  "statusCode": 200,
                  "body": json.dumps({"message": "Hello from Lambda non-proxy integration"})
              }

  ApiGatewayRestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: NonProxyIntegrationAPI
      Description: API Gateway REST API with Lambda non-proxy integration.
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
        - Key: IntegrationType
          Value: NonProxy

  ApiGatewayResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      ParentId: !GetAtt ApiGatewayRestApi.RootResourceId
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      PathPart: myresource

  ApiGatewayMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref ApiGatewayResource
      HttpMethod: POST
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${LambdaFunction.Arn}/invocations
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json: |
            {
              "body": "$input.body",
              "headers": {
                #foreach($header in $input.params().header.keySet())
                "$header": "$input.params().header.get($header)"#if($foreach.hasNext),#end
                #end
              },
              "queryStringParameters": {
                #foreach($query in $input.params().querystring.keySet())
                "$query": "$input.params().querystring.get($query)"#if($foreach.hasNext),#end
                #end
              },
              "pathParameters": {
                #foreach($param in $input.params().path.keySet())
                "$param": "$input.params().path.get($param)"#if($foreach.hasNext),#end
                #end
              },
              "httpMethod": "$context.httpMethod"
            }
        IntegrationResponses:
          - StatusCode: 200

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "Deployment of AWS Glue resources for cataloging CloudTrail logs for Athena querying."

Resources:
  GlueCrawlerRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: glue.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: GlueCrawlerPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:ListBucket
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource:
                  - "arn:aws:s3:::kk-admin"
                  - "arn:aws:s3:::kk-admin/*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - glue:CreateTable
                  - glue:UpdateTable
                  - glue:GetDatabase
                  - glue:GetTables
                  - glue:DeleteTable
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*"

  GlueDatabase:
    Type: AWS::Glue::Database
    Properties:
      CatalogId: !Ref AWS::AccountId
      DatabaseInput:
        Name: !Sub "cloudtrail_logs_${AWS::Region}"

  GlueCrawler:
    Type: AWS::Glue::Crawler
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "cloudtrail_crawler_${AWS::Region}"
      Role: !GetAtt GlueCrawlerRole.Arn
      DatabaseName: !Ref GlueDatabase
      Targets:
        S3Targets:
          - Path: "s3://kk-admin/cloud-trail/AWSLogs/"
      TablePrefix: "cloudtrail_"
      SchemaChangePolicy:
        UpdateBehavior: "UPDATE_IN_DATABASE"
        DeleteBehavior: "LOG"

Outputs:

    Description: "The name of the AWS Glue Database"
    Value: !Ref GlueDatabase
  GlueCrawlerName:
    Description: "The name of the AWS Glue Crawler"
    Value: !Ref GlueCrawler

Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create multi-threshold AWS billing alarms using CloudWatch and SNS.
  Four CloudWatch alarms monitor the EstimatedCharges metric at thresholds of $5, $10, $20, and $40.
  An SNS topic with an email subscription (xxxxx@gmail.com) is configured for alarm notifications.
Resources:
  BillingAlertSNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: BillingAlertSNSTopic
      Subscription:
        - Protocol: email
          Endpoint: xxxxx@gmail.com

  BillingAlarm5:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: "BillingAlarm-5USD"
      AlarmDescription: "Alarm when AWS EstimatedCharges exceed $5."
      Namespace: AWS/Billing
      MetricName: EstimatedCharges
      Dimensions:
        - Name: Currency
          Value: USD
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 21600
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 5
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref BillingAlertSNSTopic

  BillingAlarm10:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: "BillingAlarm-10USD"
      AlarmDescription: "Alarm when AWS EstimatedCharges exceed $10."
      Namespace: AWS/Billing
      MetricName: EstimatedCharges
      Dimensions:
        - Name: Currency
          Value: USD
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 21600
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 10
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref BillingAlertSNSTopic

  BillingAlarm20:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: "BillingAlarm-20USD"
      AlarmDescription: "Alarm when AWS EstimatedCharges exceed $20."
      Namespace: AWS/Billing
      MetricName: EstimatedCharges
      Dimensions:
        - Name: Currency
          Value: USD
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 21600
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 20
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref BillingAlertSNSTopic

  BillingAlarm40:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: "BillingAlarm-40USD"
      AlarmDescription: "Alarm when AWS EstimatedCharges exceed $40."
      Namespace: AWS/Billing
      MetricName: EstimatedCharges
      Dimensions:
        - Name: Currency
          Value: USD
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 21600
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 40
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref BillingAlertSNSTopic

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template integrating DynamoDB Streams with Amazon EventBridge via EventBridge Pipes.
  The template creates a DynamoDB table with streams enabled, an EventBridge event bus, an IAM role with least-privilege permissions,
  and an EventBridge Pipe that filters stream events based on the "avatarUrl" attribute ending with ".png" and routes matching events
  to the event bus with predefined metadata (DetailType and Source).

Parameters:
  Env:
    Description: "Environment identifier (e.g., dev, prod)"
    Type: String
    Default: "dev"

Resources:
  UsersTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub "users-table-${Env}"
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: UserId
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: UserId
          KeyType: HASH
      StreamSpecification:
        StreamViewType: NEW_IMAGE
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Env
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "UsersTable-${Env}"

  UsersEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "users-event-bus-${Env}"
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Env
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "UsersEventBus-${Env}"

  PipesRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "pipes-role-${Env}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: pipes.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: DynamoDBStreamAndEventBridgeAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - dynamodb:DescribeStream
                  - dynamodb:GetRecords
                  - dynamodb:GetShardIterator
                  - dynamodb:ListStreams
                Resource: !GetAtt UsersTable.StreamArn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource: !GetAtt UsersEventBus.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Env
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "PipesRole-${Env}"

  DynamoDBToEventBridgePipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "dynamodb-to-eventbridge-pipe-${Env}"
      RoleArn: !GetAtt PipesRole.Arn
      Source: !GetAtt UsersTable.StreamArn
      SourceParameters:
        DynamoDBStreamParameters:
          BatchSize: 10
          StartingPosition: TRIM_HORIZON
        FilterCriteria:
          Filters:
            - Pattern: "{\"dynamodb\":{\"NewImage\":{\"avatarUrl\":{\"S\":[{\"suffix\": \".png\"}]}}}}"
      Target: !GetAtt UsersEventBus.Arn
      TargetParameters:
        EventBridgeEventBusParameters:
          DetailType: "UserAvatarUpdated"
          Source: "com.mycompany.users"
      Tags:
        Environment: !Ref Env
        Name: !Sub "DDBtoEBPipe-${Env}"

Outputs:
  DynamoDBTableName:
    Description: "Name of the DynamoDB table"
    Value: !Ref UsersTable
  DynamoDBTableStreamArn:
    Description: "Stream ARN of the DynamoDB table"
    Value: !GetAtt UsersTable.StreamArn
  EventBusName:
    Description: "Name of the EventBridge event bus"
    Value: !Ref UsersEventBus
  EventBridgePipeName:
    Description: "Name of the EventBridge Pipe"
    Value: !Ref DynamoDBToEventBridgePipe

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template creating an EventBridge configuration that routes custom events
  from a dedicated Event Bus to an external API endpoint ('https://webhook.site/testtest')
  via an API Destination secured with an API key-based Connection.
  
Parameters:
  ApiKeySecretName:
    Type: String
    Default: "MyAPIKeySecret"
    Description: "Name of the AWS Secrets Manager secret that contains the API key under the key 'ApiKey'."

Resources:
  ApiKeySecretResource:
    Type: AWS::SecretsManager::Secret
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref ApiKeySecretName
      Description: "API key for authenticating with the external API endpoint."
      SecretString: '{"ApiKey": "your-api-key-value"}'

  CustomEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EventBus"

  ApiConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ApiConnection"
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: "x-api-key"
          ApiKeyValue: !Sub "{{resolve:secretsmanager:${ApiKeySecretName}:SecretString:ApiKey}}"

  ApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ApiDestination"
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt ApiConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: "https://webhook.site/testtest"
      HttpMethod: POST

  ApiDestinationInvocationRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ApiDestinationInvokeRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeApiDestinationPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: "events:InvokeApiDestination"
                Resource: "*"

  CustomEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-Rule"
      EventBusName: !Ref CustomEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "custom.event"
      Targets:
        - Id: "ApiDestinationTarget"
          Arn: !GetAtt ApiDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt ApiDestinationInvocationRole.Arn

Outputs:
  EventBusArn:
    Description: "ARN of the EventBridge Event Bus"
    Value: !GetAtt CustomEventBus.Arn

  ConnectionArn:
    Description: "ARN of the EventBridge Connection"
    Value: !GetAtt ApiConnection.Arn

  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: "ARN of the EventBridge API Destination"
    Value: !GetAtt ApiDestination.Arn

  InvocationRoleArn:
    Description: "ARN of the IAM Role for invoking the API Destination"
    Value: !GetAtt ApiDestinationInvocationRole.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a CloudWatch Dashboard and CloudWatch Logs Insights queries
  for monitoring AWS Client VPN usage patterns. The solution aggregates metrics such as connection durations,
  data transfer volumes (ingress and egress), and user activity across different authentication methods
  (AD/SAML, Mutual Auth, and Mixed Auth). It organizes queries using naming conventions and displays
  endpoint-specific usage, unique user statistics, and authentication breakdowns in a centralized dashboard.

Resources:
  ClientVPNDashboard:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Dashboard
    Properties:
      DashboardName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ClientVPN-Dashboard"
      DashboardBody: !Sub |
        {
          "widgets": [
            {
              "type": "log",
              "x": 0,
              "y": 0,
              "width": 12,
              "height": 6,
              "properties": {
                "queryDefinitionId": "${AdSamlQuery}",
                "title": "Endpoint Specific Usage (AD/SAML)",
                "region": "${AWS::Region}"
              }
            },
            {
              "type": "log",
              "x": 12,
              "y": 0,
              "width": 12,
              "height": 6,
              "properties": {
                "queryDefinitionId": "${MutualAuthQuery}",
                "title": "Distinct User Statistics (Mutual Auth)",
                "region": "${AWS::Region}"
              }
            },
            {
              "type": "log",
              "x": 0,
              "y": 6,
              "width": 24,
              "height": 6,
              "properties": {
                "queryDefinitionId": "${MixedAuthQuery}",
                "title": "Authentication Type Breakdown (Mixed Auth)",
                "region": "${AWS::Region}"
              }
            }
          ]
        }

  AdSamlQuery:
    Type: AWS::Logs::QueryDefinition
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-AD-SAML-Usage"
      QueryString: |
        fields @timestamp, endpoint, clientIp, connectionDuration, ingress, egress, user
        | filter authMethod = 'AD/SAML'
        | stats sum(connectionDuration) as TotalDuration, sum(ingress) as TotalIngress, sum(egress) as TotalEgress, count_distinct(user) as UniqueUsers by endpoint
      LogGroupNames:
        - ClientVPNLogs

  MutualAuthQuery:
    Type: AWS::Logs::QueryDefinition
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-MutualAuth-Usage"
      QueryString: |
        fields @timestamp, endpoint, clientIp, connectionDuration, ingress, egress, user
        | filter authMethod = 'Mutual Auth'
        | stats sum(connectionDuration) as TotalDuration, sum(ingress) as TotalIngress, sum(egress) as TotalEgress, count_distinct(user) as UniqueUsers by endpoint
      LogGroupNames:
        - ClientVPNLogs

  MixedAuthQuery:
    Type: AWS::Logs::QueryDefinition
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-MixedAuth-Usage"
      QueryString: |
        fields @timestamp, endpoint, clientIp, connectionDuration, ingress, egress, user, authMethod
        | filter authMethod = 'Mixed Auth'
        | stats sum(connectionDuration) as TotalDuration, sum(ingress) as TotalIngress, sum(egress) as TotalEgress, count_distinct(user) as UniqueUsers by endpoint, authMethod
      LogGroupNames:
        - ClientVPNLogs

Outputs:
  DashboardURL:
    Description: "URL of the CloudWatch Dashboard"
    Value: !Sub "https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/home?region=${AWS::Region}#dashboards:name=${ClientVPNDashboard}"
  AdSamlQuery:
    Description: "QueryDefinition ID for AD/SAML Usage Query"
    Value: !Ref AdSamlQuery
  MutualAuthQuery:
    Description: "QueryDefinition ID for Mutual Auth Usage Query"
    Value: !Ref MutualAuthQuery
  MixedAuthQuery:

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: CloudFormation template to create a DynamoDB table with Local and Global Secondary Indexes.

Resources:
  DynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-DynamoTable"
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: UserId
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: Timestamp
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: LSI_Sort
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: GSI_PK
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: GSI_SK
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: UserId
          KeyType: HASH
        - AttributeName: Timestamp
          KeyType: RANGE
      LocalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LSI"
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: UserId
              KeyType: HASH
            - AttributeName: LSI_Sort
              KeyType: RANGE
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL
      GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-GSI"
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: GSI_PK
              KeyType: HASH
            - AttributeName: GSI_SK
              KeyType: RANGE
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL

Outputs:
  TableName:
    Description: "DynamoDB Table Name"
    Value: !Ref DynamoDBTable
  TableArn:
    Description: "DynamoDB Table ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt DynamoDBTable.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFormation template to create a DynamoDB table with PAY_PER_REQUEST billing, server-side encryption, and a Retain deletion policy.

Parameters:
  TableName:
    Type: String
    Default: MyDynamoDBTable
    Description: The name of the DynamoDB table.
  PartitionKeyName:
    Type: String
    Default: PK
    Description: The attribute name for the partition key.
  PartitionKeyType:
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - S
      - N
      - B
    Default: S
    Description: The data type for the partition key.
  UseSortKey:
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - true
      - false
    Default: false
    Description: Specify true to use a sort key, false otherwise.
  SortKeyName:
    Type: String
    Default: SK
    Description: The attribute name for the sort key (only used if UseSortKey is true).
  SortKeyType:
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - S
      - N
      - B
    Default: S
    Description: The data type for the sort key.

Conditions:
  IncludeSortKey: !Equals [!Ref UseSortKey, "true"]

Resources:
  DynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
    Properties:
      TableName: !Ref TableName
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions: !If
        - IncludeSortKey
        - 
          - AttributeName: !Ref PartitionKeyName
            AttributeType: !Ref PartitionKeyType
          - AttributeName: !Ref SortKeyName
            AttributeType: !Ref SortKeyType
        - 
          - AttributeName: !Ref PartitionKeyName
            AttributeType: !Ref PartitionKeyType
      KeySchema: !If
        - IncludeSortKey
        - 
          - AttributeName: !Ref PartitionKeyName
            KeyType: HASH
          - AttributeName: !Ref SortKeyName
            KeyType: RANGE
        - 
          - AttributeName: !Ref PartitionKeyName
            KeyType: HASH
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true

Outputs:
  DynamoDBTableName:
    Description: The name of the DynamoDB table.
    Value: !Ref DynamoDBTable
  DynamoDBTableArn:
    Description: The ARN of the DynamoDB table.
    Value: !GetAtt DynamoDBTable.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template creating a VPC with two subnets, an Auto Scaling Group with cost-effective instances and Internet access,
  an ElastiCache replication group for Redis with automated snapshot management and failover,
  plus a Lambda function and its IAM role to enable/disable snapshotting dynamically.

Parameters:
  VpcCIDR:
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16
  PublicSubnet1CIDR:
    Description: CIDR block for the first public subnet
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.1.0/24
  PublicSubnet2CIDR:
    Description: CIDR block for the second public subnet
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.2.0/24
  AutoScalingInstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type for the Auto Scaling Group instances
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
  ElastiCacheNodeType:
    Description: ElastiCache node instance type
    Type: String
    Default: cache.t3.micro
  SnapshotRetentionLimit:
    Description: Number of days to retain ElastiCache snapshots (0 disables snapshots)
    Type: Number
    Default: 7
  SnapshotWindow:
    Description: Daily snapshot window in UTC (e.g., 05:00-09:00)
    Type: String
    Default: '05:00-09:00'

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCIDR
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PublicSubnet1CIDR
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet1"

  PublicSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PublicSubnet2CIDR
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet2"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicRouteTable"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  LaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LaunchTemplate"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: ami-0c94855ba95c71c99  # Example Amazon Linux 2 AMI; update as needed per region.
        InstanceType: !Ref AutoScalingInstanceType
        NetworkInterfaces:
          - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
            DeviceIndex: 0

  AutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: '1'
      MaxSize: '3'
      DesiredCapacity: '1'
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ASG"
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

  ElastiCacheSubnetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElastiCache::SubnetGroup
    Properties:
      Description: "Subnet group for ElastiCache replication group"
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      CacheSubnetGroupName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ElastiCacheSubnetGroup"

  ElastiCacheReplicationGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup
    Properties:
      ReplicationGroupDescription: !Sub "${AWS::StackName} Redis replication group"
      Engine: redis
      CacheNodeType: !Ref ElastiCacheNodeType
      NumCacheClusters: 2
      AutomaticFailoverEnabled: true
      SnapshotRetentionLimit: !Ref SnapshotRetentionLimit
      SnapshotWindow: !Ref SnapshotWindow
      CacheSubnetGroupName: !Ref ElastiCacheSubnetGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ElastiCache"

  SnapshotManagementLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaElastiCachePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - elasticache:ModifyReplicationGroup
                  - elasticache:DescribeReplicationGroups
                  - elasticache:ListTagsForResource
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*"

  SnapshotManagementLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt SnapshotManagementLambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import os
          import boto3

          def handler(event, context):
              action = event.get("action", "disable")
              client = boto3.client('elasticache')
              replication_group_id = os.environ.get("REPLICATION_GROUP_ID")
              if not replication_group_id:
                  return {"status": "error", "message": "Replication group ID not provided."}
              try:
                  if action == "enable":
                      snapshot_retention = int(os.environ.get("SNAPSHOT_RETENTION_LIMIT", "7"))
                  else:
                      snapshot_retention = 0
                  response = client.modify_replication_group(
                      ReplicationGroupId=replication_group_id,
                      SnapshotRetentionLimit=snapshot_retention,
                      ApplyImmediately=True
                  )
                  return {"status": "success", "response": response}
              except Exception as e:
                  return {"status": "error", "message": str(e)}
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPLICATION_GROUP_ID: !Ref ElastiCacheReplicationGroup
          SNAPSHOT_RETENTION_LIMIT: !Ref SnapshotRetentionLimit

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: VPC ID
    Value: !Ref VPC

  PublicSubnet1Id:
    Description: Public Subnet 1 ID
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet1

  PublicSubnet2Id:
    Description: Public Subnet 2 ID
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet2

  AutoScalingGroupName:
    Description: Auto Scaling Group name
    Value: !Ref AutoScalingGroup

  ElastiCachePrimaryEndpoint:
    Description: ElastiCache replication group primary endpoint
    Value: !GetAtt ElastiCacheReplicationGroup.PrimaryEndPoint.Address

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that creates an IoT data pipeline routing messages from AWS IoT Core to an SQS queue via SNS.
  It creates an SQS queue for storing messages, an SNS topic for pub/sub, and an IoT Topic Rule that triggers on messages
  published to the MQTT topic "device/data". Necessary permissions are configured for AWS IoT to send messages to SNS.

Resources:
  IoTRuleRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: IoTToSNSRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: iot.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: IoTToSNSPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sns:Publish
                Resource: !Ref SNSTopic

  SQSQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: IoTDataQueue

  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: IoTDataTopic

  SNSTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref SNSTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowIoTPublish
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: iot.amazonaws.com
            Action: sns:Publish
            Resource: !Ref SNSTopic

  IoTTopicRule:
    Type: AWS::IoT::TopicRule
    Properties:
      RuleName: IoTToSNSTopicRule
      TopicRulePayload:
        Sql: "SELECT * FROM 'device/data'"
        AwsIotSqlVersion: "2016-03-23"
        RuleDisabled: false
        Actions:
          - Sns:
              TargetArn: !Ref SNSTopic
              RoleArn: !GetAtt IoTRuleRole.Arn

Outputs:

    Description: URL of the SQS Queue storing IoT messages
    Value: !Ref SQSQueue
  SNSTopicArn:
    Description: ARN of the SNS Topic used as intermediary
    Value: !Ref SNSTopic
  IoTTopicRuleName:
    Description: Name of the IoT Topic Rule
    Value: !Ref IoTTopicRule
  IoTTopicRuleArn:
    Description: ARN of the IoT Topic Rule
    Value: !Sub "arn:aws:iot:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:rule/${IoTTopicRule}"

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an S3 bucket,
  an SNS Topic for notifications, and an EventBridge rule
  that triggers on S3 Object Created events and sends them to the SNS Topic.

Resources:
  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-s3bucket-${AWS::Region}"

  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-s3topic"

  SNSTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref SNSTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowEventBridgePublish
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sns:Publish
            Resource: !Ref SNSTopic

  EventBridgeRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-s3event-rule"
      Description: "Triggers on S3 Object Created events and forwards them to the SNS Topic."
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "aws.s3"
        detail-type:
          - "Object Created"
        detail:
          bucket:
            name:
              - !Ref S3Bucket
      Targets:
        - Id: SNSTarget
          Arn: !Ref SNSTopic

Outputs:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 Bucket"
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket

  SNSTopicARN:
    Description: "ARN of the SNS Topic"
    Value: !Ref SNSTopic

  EventBridgeRuleName:
    Description: "Name of the EventBridge Rule"
    Value: !Ref EventBridgeRule

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Real-time data ingestion and storage pipeline using Amazon Kinesis Data Stream for real-time ingestion,
  Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose for streaming transformation/delivery, and an Amazon S3 Bucket for durable storage.

Parameters:
  KinesisShards:
    Description: Number of shards for the Kinesis Data Stream.
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
    MinValue: 1
  RetentionPeriodHours:
    Description: Data retention period for the Kinesis Data Stream (in hours).
    Type: Number
    Default: 24
    MinValue: 24
  FirehoseBufferSizeInMBs:
    Description: Buffer size in MBs for Kinesis Data Firehose before data is delivered to S3.
    Type: Number
    Default: 5
  FirehoseBufferIntervalInSeconds:
    Description: Buffer interval in seconds for Kinesis Data Firehose before data is delivered to S3.
    Type: Number
    Default: 300

Resources:
  KinesisDataStream:
    Type: AWS::Kinesis::Stream
    Properties:
      ShardCount: !Ref KinesisShards
      RetentionPeriodHours: !Ref RetentionPeriodHours

  S3DataBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  FirehoseS3Role:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "FirehoseS3Role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: firehose.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: FirehoseS3AccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:AbortMultipartUpload
                  - s3:GetBucketLocation
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "${S3DataBucket.Arn}"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:PutObjectAcl
                  - s3:PutObjectTagging
                  - s3:PutObjectVersionAcl
                  - s3:PutObjectVersionTagging
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "${S3DataBucket.Arn}/*"

  KinesisFirehoseDeliveryStream:
    Type: AWS::KinesisFirehose::DeliveryStream
    Properties:
      DeliveryStreamName: !Sub "KinesisFirehoseStream-${AWS::StackName}"
      DeliveryStreamType: DirectPut
      S3DestinationConfiguration:
        BucketARN: !GetAtt S3DataBucket.Arn
        RoleARN: !GetAtt FirehoseS3Role.Arn
        BufferingHints:
          SizeInMBs: !Ref FirehoseBufferSizeInMBs
          IntervalInSeconds: !Ref FirehoseBufferIntervalInSeconds
        CompressionFormat: UNCOMPRESSED

Outputs:
  KinesisDataStreamARN:
    Description: ARN of the Kinesis Data Stream.
    Value: !GetAtt KinesisDataStream.Arn
  S3BucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 Bucket used for data storage.
    Value: !Ref S3DataBucket
  FirehoseDeliveryStreamName:
    Description: Name of the Kinesis Data Firehose Delivery Stream.
    Value: !Ref KinesisFirehoseDeliveryStream

        Statement:

            Action: "SNS:Publish"
            Resource: !Ref SecretsLifecycleTopic

    Properties:
      Description: "EventBridge rule to capture AWS Secrets Manager lifecycle events (CreateSecret, UpdateSecret, GetSecretValue) and send notifications via SNS."
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "aws.secretsmanager"
        detail-type:
          - "AWS API Call via CloudTrail"
        detail:
          eventName:

            - "UpdateSecret"
            - "GetSecretValue"
      Targets:
        - Id: "SecretsLifecycleSNSTarget"
          Arn: !Ref SecretsLifecycleTopic

Outputs:

    Description: "ARN of the SNS Topic for AWS Secrets Manager lifecycle events notifications"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create secure parameter storage and retrieval workflows using AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store,
  AWS Secrets Manager, and a Lambda function that fetches and decrypts these values. Resources include a stack namespaced SSM parameter,
  a Secrets Manager secret with a generated password, a Lambda function in python3.12 demonstrating Boto3 usage,
  and an IAM role with least-privilege permissions.

Parameters:
  LambdaTimeout:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    Description: Lambda function timeout in seconds

Resources:

  SSMParameter:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "/${AWS::StackName}/config/value"
      Type: String
      Value: "This is A/B"

  SecretsSecret:
    Type: AWS::SecretsManager::Secret
    Properties:
      Description: "Secret containing a predefined username and a dynamically generated password"
      GenerateSecretString:
        SecretStringTemplate: '{"username": "admin"}'
        GenerateStringKey: "password"
        ExcludeCharacters: '"@/\\'
        PasswordLength: 16

  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaSSMSecretsPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:GetParameter
                  - ssm:GetParametersByPath
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/${AWS::StackName}/*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - secretsmanager:GetSecretValue
                Resource: !Ref SecretsSecret
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/lambda/*"

  LambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SSMSecretsLambda"
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: !Ref LambdaTimeout
      Environment:
        Variables:
          STACK_NAME: !Ref AWS::StackName
          SSM_PARAMETER_PATH: !Sub "/${AWS::StackName}/config/"
          SECRET_ARN: !Ref SecretsSecret
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import os
          import boto3
          import json

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Initialize clients
              ssm = boto3.client('ssm')
              secretsmanager = boto3.client('secretsmanager')

              # Retrieve parameters from SSM Parameter Store using a path-based query
              parameter_path = os.environ.get('SSM_PARAMETER_PATH')
              ssm_response = ssm.get_parameters_by_path(
                  Path=parameter_path,
                  Recursive=True,
                  WithDecryption=True
              )
              
              # Fetch the secret value from Secrets Manager
              secret_arn = os.environ.get('SECRET_ARN')
              secret_response = secretsmanager.get_secret_value(SecretId=secret_arn)
              secret_string = secret_response.get('SecretString', '')

              # Return the collected data
              return {
                  'statusCode': 200,
                  'SSMParameters': ssm_response.get('Parameters', []),
                  'Secret': secret_string
              }

Outputs:
  SSMParameterName:
    Description: "Name of the SSM Parameter created"
    Value: !Ref SSMParameter

  SecretArn:
    Description: "ARN of the Secrets Manager secret"
    Value: !Ref SecretsSecret

  LambdaFunctionName:
    Description: "Name of the Lambda Function deployed"
    Value: !Ref LambdaFunction

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy an encrypted EFS file system with mount targets in two public subnets,
  and two EC2 instances in different Availability Zones that automatically mount the EFS file system.
  The solution includes a VPC with public subnets, an Internet Gateway, a network ACL,
  security groups with controlled NFS access, IAM roles with proper permissions,
  CloudFormation wait conditions using cfn-signal, and CloudWatch alarms for EFS performance.

Parameters:
  KeyName:

Mappings:
  RegionMap:
    us-east-1:

    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-0b69ea66ff7391e80
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-08962a4068733a2b6

Resources:

  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EFS-MultiAZ-VPC

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EFS-IGW

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet-1

  PublicSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet-2

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicRouteTable

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  VPCNetworkAcl:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAcl
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EFS-NetworkACL

  InboundNACLRuleHTTP:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref VPCNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 100
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      PortRange:
        From: 80
        To: 80

  InboundNACLRuleNFS:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref VPCNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 110
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: !GetAtt VPC.CidrBlock
      PortRange:
        From: 2049
        To: 2049

  OutboundNACLRuleAll:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref VPCNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 100
      Protocol: -1
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: true
      CidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0

  SubnetNetworkAclAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetNetworkAclAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      NetworkAclId: !Ref VPCNetworkAcl

  SubnetNetworkAclAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetNetworkAclAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      NetworkAclId: !Ref VPCNetworkAcl

  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for EC2 instances with SSM and SSH access
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-InstanceSG

  EFSSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for EFS mount targets allowing inbound NFS access from EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 2049
          ToPort: 2049
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EFS-SG

  EFSFileSystem:
    Type: AWS::EFS::FileSystem
    Properties:
      Encrypted: true
      PerformanceMode: generalPurpose
      FileSystemTags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: MultiAZEFS

  EFSMountTargetSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EFS::MountTarget
    Properties:
      FileSystemId: !Ref EFSFileSystem
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref EFSSecurityGroup

  EFSMountTargetSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EFS::MountTarget
    Properties:
      FileSystemId: !Ref EFSFileSystem
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref EFSSecurityGroup

  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EFSAccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - elasticfilesystem:ClientMount
                  - elasticfilesystem:ClientWrite
                  - elasticfilesystem:DescribeMountTargets
                Resource: '*'

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole
      Path: /

  EC2Instance1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Metadata:
      AWS::CloudFormation::Init:
        config:
          packages:
            yum:
              amazon-efs-utils: []
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: !FindInMap [RegionMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", AMI]
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: '0'
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
          SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      UserData:
        'Fn::Base64': !Sub
          - |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y
            yum install -y amazon-efs-utils aws-cfn-bootstrap
            FilesystemId=${FilesystemId}
            MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/efs"
            mkdir -p $$MOUNT_POINT
            RETRIES=0
            MAX_RETRIES=30
            while ! mount -t efs -o tls ${FilesystemId}:/ $$MOUNT_POINT && [ $$RETRIES -lt $$MAX_RETRIES ]; do
              echo "Mount target not ready, retrying in 10 seconds..."
              sleep 10
              RETRIES=$$(( $$RETRIES + 1 ))
            done
            if mountpoint -q $$MOUNT_POINT; then
              /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal --exit-code 0 --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource EC2Instance1 --region ${AWS::Region}
            else
              /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal --exit-code 1 --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource EC2Instance1 --region ${AWS::Region}
            fi
          - {FilesystemId: !Ref EFSFileSystem}
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-Instance-1
    CreationPolicy:
      ResourceSignal:
        Count: 1
        Timeout: PT10M

  EC2Instance2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Metadata:
      AWS::CloudFormation::Init:
        config:
          packages:
            yum:
              amazon-efs-utils: []
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: !FindInMap [RegionMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", AMI]
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: '0'
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
          SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      UserData:
        'Fn::Base64': !Sub
          - |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y
            yum install -y amazon-efs-utils aws-cfn-bootstrap
            FilesystemId=${FilesystemId}
            MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/efs"
            mkdir -p $$MOUNT_POINT
            RETRIES=0
            MAX_RETRIES=30
            while ! mount -t efs -o tls ${FilesystemId}:/ $$MOUNT_POINT && [ $$RETRIES -lt $$MAX_RETRIES ]; do
              echo "Mount target not ready, retrying in 10 seconds..."
              sleep 10
              RETRIES=$$(( $$RETRIES + 1 ))
            done
            if mountpoint -q $$MOUNT_POINT; then
              /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal --exit-code 0 --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource EC2Instance2 --region ${AWS::Region}
            else
              /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal --exit-code 1 --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource EC2Instance2 --region ${AWS::Region}
            fi
          - {FilesystemId: !Ref EFSFileSystem}
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-Instance-2
    CreationPolicy:
      ResourceSignal:
        Count: 1
        Timeout: PT10M

  EFSIOAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmDescription: Alarm when EFS I/O usage exceeds 95% of its limit.
      Namespace: AWS/EFS
      MetricName: PercentIOLimit
      Dimensions:
        - Name: FileSystemId
          Value: !Ref EFSFileSystem
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 95
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold
      AlarmActions: []

  EFSThroughputAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmDescription: Alarm when EFS throughput utilization exceeds 80%.
      Namespace: AWS/EFS
      MetricName: PercentThroughputUtilization
      Dimensions:
        - Name: FileSystemId
          Value: !Ref EFSFileSystem
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 80
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold
      AlarmActions: []

Outputs:
  EFSFileSystemId:
    Description: The ID of the EFS file system.
    Value: !Ref EFSFileSystem
  EC2Instance1Id:
    Description: The ID of the first EC2 instance.
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance1
  EC2Instance2Id:
    Description: The ID of the second EC2 instance.
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance2

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation Template to create an automated deployment pipeline for the Etherpad application.
  This template provisions:
    - An S3 Bucket for storing deployment artifacts with a unique name.
    - An Auto Scaling Group (spanning two subnets provided via parameters) with a Launch Template that installs Node.js and the CodeDeploy agent.
    - An internet‐facing Application Load Balancer with Listener and Target Group.
    - A secure MySQL RDS instance for Etherpad database storage.
    - AWS Systems Manager (SSM) Parameter Store entry for secure database credentials.
    - AWS CodeDeploy Application and Deployment Group integrated with the Auto Scaling Group.
    - Necessary IAM roles and instance profiles for EC2 and CodeDeploy.
Metadata:
  AWS::CloudFormation::Interface:
    ParameterGroups:
      - Label:
          default: "Network Configuration"
        Parameters:
          - VPCId
          - Subnet1
          - Subnet2
      - Label:
          default: "EC2 Configuration"
        Parameters:
          - InstanceType
          - KeyName
      - Label:
          default: "Database Configuration"
        Parameters:
          - DBMasterUsername
          - DBMasterUserPassword
    ParameterLabels:
      VPCId:
        default: "VPC ID"
      Subnet1:
        default: "First Subnet ID"
      Subnet2:
        default: "Second Subnet ID"
      InstanceType:
        default: "EC2 Instance Type"
      KeyName:
        default: "Key Pair Name"
      DBMasterUsername:
        default: "Database Admin Username"
      DBMasterUserPassword:
        default: "Database Admin Password"
Parameters:
  VPCId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: "The VPC ID in which resources will be deployed."
    Default: "vpc-12345678"
  Subnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: "The first subnet ID for the Auto Scaling Group."
    Default: "subnet-12345678"
  Subnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: "The second subnet ID for the Auto Scaling Group."
    Default: "subnet-87654321"
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    Description: "EC2 instance type for Etherpad application."
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: "Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instances."
    Default: "my-key-pair"
  DBMasterUsername:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Description: "The database admin account username"
    Default: "etherpadadmin"
  DBMasterUserPassword:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Description: "The database admin account password"
    Default: "ChangeMe!"
Resources:
  ArtifactBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "etherpad-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: Etherpad

  ArtifactBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref ArtifactBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowInternalBucketAccess
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action:
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:GetObject
            Resource: !Sub "${ArtifactBucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: false

  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EtherpadEC2Role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: [ec2.amazonaws.com]
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonEC2RoleforAWSCodeDeploy
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "EtherpadInstanceProfile-${AWS::StackName}"
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  CodeDeployServiceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EtherpadCodeDeployRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: [codedeploy.amazonaws.com]
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CodeDeployPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - autoscaling:CompleteLifecycleAction
                  - autoscaling:DeleteLifecycleHook
                  - autoscaling:DescribeAutoScalingGroups
                  - autoscaling:DescribeLifecycleHooks
                  - autoscaling:PutLifecycleHook
                  - autoscaling:RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - elasticloadbalancing:Describe*
                  - elasticloadbalancing:ModifyTargetGroup
                  - elasticloadbalancing:ModifyListener
                  - elasticloadbalancing:RegisterTargets
                  - elasticloadbalancing:DeregisterTargets
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:Get*
                  - s3:List*
                Resource: "*"

  LaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "EtherpadLaunchTemplate-${AWS::StackName}"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Name: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
        ImageId: ami-0c94855ba95c71c99
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y
            # Install Node.js
            curl --silent --location https://rpm.nodesource.com/setup_14.x | bash -
            yum install -y nodejs
            # Install CodeDeploy Agent
            yum install -y ruby wget
            cd /home/ec2-user
            wget https://aws-codedeploy-${AWS::Region}.s3.amazonaws.com/latest/install
            chmod +x ./install
            ./install auto
            systemctl start codedeploy-agent
            # Signal CloudFormation that the instance is ready
            /opt/aws/bin/cfn-signal -e $? --stack ${AWS::StackName} --resource AutoScalingGroup --region ${AWS::Region}

  AutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      AutoScalingGroupName: !Sub "EtherpadASG-${AWS::StackName}"
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref Subnet1
        - !Ref Subnet2
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: '1'
      MaxSize: '3'
      DesiredCapacity: '1'
      TargetGroupARNs:
        - !Ref TargetGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EtherpadInstance
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

  ALB:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "EtherpadALB-${AWS::StackName}"
      Scheme: internet-facing
      Subnets:
        - !Ref Subnet1
        - !Ref Subnet2
      SecurityGroups: []
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: Etherpad

  TargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "EtherpadTG-${AWS::StackName}"
      Port: 80
      Protocol: HTTP
      VpcId: !Ref VPCId
      TargetType: instance
      HealthCheckProtocol: HTTP
      HealthCheckPort: '80'
      HealthCheckPath: /
      Matcher:
        HttpCode: '200'
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: Etherpad

  ALBListener:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref ALB
      Port: 80
      Protocol: HTTP
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref TargetGroup

  RDSSubnetGroup:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBSubnetGroup
    Properties:
      DBSubnetGroupDescription: "Subnets for Etherpad RDS instance"
      SubnetIds:
        - !Ref Subnet1
        - !Ref Subnet2
      DBSubnetGroupName: !Sub "etherpad-rds-subnet-${AWS::StackName}"

  EtherpadDB:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      DBInstanceIdentifier: !Sub "etherpad-db-${AWS::StackName}"
      AllocatedStorage: 20
      DBInstanceClass: db.t3.micro
      Engine: MySQL
      EngineVersion: "8.0.45"
      MasterUsername: !Ref DBMasterUsername
      MasterUserPassword: !Ref DBMasterUserPassword
      DBSubnetGroupName: !Ref RDSSubnetGroup
      VPCSecurityGroups: []
      MultiAZ: false
      StorageEncrypted: true
      BackupRetentionPeriod: 7
      PubliclyAccessible: false

  DBMasterUsernameParameter:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "/etherpad/db/master/username-${AWS::StackName}"
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref DBMasterUsername
      Description: "Master username for Etherpad RDS instance."

  CodeDeployApplication:
    Type: AWS::CodeDeploy::Application
    Properties:
      ApplicationName: !Sub "EtherpadApp-${AWS::StackName}"
      ComputePlatform: Server

  CodeDeployDeploymentGroup:
    Type: AWS::CodeDeploy::DeploymentGroup
    Properties:
      ApplicationName: !Ref CodeDeployApplication
      DeploymentGroupName: !Sub "EtherpadDG-${AWS::StackName}"
      ServiceRoleArn: !GetAtt CodeDeployServiceRole.Arn
      DeploymentConfigName: CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime
      AutoScalingGroups:
        - !Ref AutoScalingGroup
      LoadBalancerInfo:
        TargetGroupPairInfoList:
          - TargetGroups:
              - Name: !GetAtt TargetGroup.TargetGroupName
            ProdTrafficRoute:
              ListenerArns:
                - !Ref ALBListener
      TriggerConfigurations: []
      AlarmConfiguration:
        Enabled: false

  The Lambda function uses Python3.12 runtime with inline code and includes basic configuration such as memory size, timeout, and tags.

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create two IAM roles for EC2 instances with read-only access 
  to IAM metadata. Each role trusts the EC2 service and includes an inline policy that 
  allows iam:Get* and iam:List* actions on all IAM resources.

Resources:
  IamReadOnlyRole1:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EC2-IAM-ReadOnly-Role-1-${AWS::AccountId}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: "sts:AssumeRole"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub "EC2-IAM-ReadOnly-Policy-1-${AWS::AccountId}"
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - "iam:Get*"
                  - "iam:List*"
                Resource: "*"

  IamReadOnlyRole2:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EC2-IAM-ReadOnly-Role-2-${AWS::AccountId}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: "sts:AssumeRole"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub "EC2-IAM-ReadOnly-Policy-2-${AWS::AccountId}"
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - "iam:Get*"
                  - "iam:List*"
                Resource: "*"

Outputs:
  IamReadOnlyRole1Arn:
    Description: ARN of the first IAM read-only role for EC2 instances.
    Value: !GetAtt IamReadOnlyRole1.Arn
  IamReadOnlyRole2Arn:
    Description: ARN of the second IAM read-only role for EC2 instances.
    Value: !GetAtt IamReadOnlyRole2.Arn
