AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a VPC, Security Group, S3 Bucket (with encryption, versioning, public access blocks and a bucket policy enforcing HTTPS)
  and a DynamoDB Table with deletion protection (Retain deletion and update/replace policies). This template follows AWS best practices.

Parameters:
  VPCCIDR:
    Description: "CIDR block for the VPC"
    Type: String
    Default: "10.0.0.0/16"

Metadata:
  AWS::CloudFormation::Interface:
    ParameterGroups:
      - Label:
          default: "VPC Configuration"
        Parameters:
          - VPCCIDR
    ParameterLabels:
      VPCCIDR:
        default: "VPC CIDR Block"

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VPCCIDR
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-vpc"

  SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security Group with minimal inbound rules"
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress: []
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: "-1"
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-sg"

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-bucket-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-s3bucket"

  S3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref S3Bucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: EnforceTLS
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:*"
            Resource:
              - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${S3Bucket}"
              - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${S3Bucket}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: false

  DynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: Id
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: Id
          KeyType: HASH
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
        PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-dynamodb"
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy an EC2 instance with a security group
  allowing HTTP access and an S3 bucket with a dynamically generated unique name.
  This template uses a Lambda-backed custom resource to look up the default VPC and one of its subnets
  from the target environment. Ensure that the Lambda execution role has sufficient permissions.

Parameters:
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
    ConstraintDescription: must be a valid EC2 instance type.

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: NetworkLookupPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:DescribeVpcs
                  - ec2:DescribeSubnets
                Resource: "*"

  DefaultNetworkLookupFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.8
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json, urllib3, boto3
          
          http = urllib3.PoolManager()
          
          def send_response(event, context, responseStatus, responseData, physicalResourceId=None, noEcho=False):
              responseUrl = event['ResponseURL']
              responseBody = {
                  'Status': responseStatus,
                  'Reason': 'See details in CloudWatch Log Stream: ' + context.log_stream_name,
                  'PhysicalResourceId': physicalResourceId or context.log_stream_name,
                  'StackId': event['StackId'],
                  'RequestId': event['RequestId'],
                  'LogicalResourceId': event['LogicalResourceId'],
                  'NoEcho': noEcho,
                  'Data': responseData
              }
              json_responseBody = json.dumps(responseBody)
              headers = {'content-type': '', 'content-length': str(len(json_responseBody))}
              http.request('PUT', responseUrl, headers=headers, body=json_responseBody)
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
              try:
                  # Get the default VPC
                  vpcs = ec2.describe_vpcs(Filters=[{'Name': 'isDefault', 'Values': ['true']}])['Vpcs']
                  if not vpcs:
                      raise Exception("No default VPC found")
                  default_vpc = vpcs[0]['VpcId']
                  # Get one subnet from the default VPC
                  subnets = ec2.describe_subnets(Filters=[{'Name': 'vpc-id', 'Values': [default_vpc]}])['Subnets']
                  if not subnets:
                      raise Exception("No subnets found in the default VPC")
                  default_subnet = subnets[0]['SubnetId']
                  responseData = {'VpcId': default_vpc, 'SubnetId': default_subnet}
                  send_response(event, context, "SUCCESS", responseData)
              except Exception as e:
                  send_response(event, context, "FAILED", {'Message': str(e)})

  DefaultNetwork:
    Type: Custom::DefaultNetwork
    DependsOn: DefaultNetworkLookupFunction
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt DefaultNetworkLookupFunction.Arn

  HttpSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow inbound HTTP access
      VpcId: !GetAtt DefaultNetwork.VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2}}"
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - DeviceIndex: "0"
          SubnetId: !GetAtt DefaultNetwork.SubnetId
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref HttpSecurityGroup
          AssociatePublicIpAddress: true

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "mybucket-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::StackName}"

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: "ID of the EC2 instance"
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  BucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 bucket"
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
  DefaultVpcId:
    Description: "Discovered default VPC ID"
    Value: !GetAtt DefaultNetwork.VpcId
  DefaultSubnetId:
    Description: "Discovered default Subnet ID"
    Value: !GetAtt DefaultNetwork.SubnetId

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy a secure, web-based VS Code development environment
  using an EC2 instance running Ubuntu 22.04 with code-server. The instance is placed in a VPC with a public subnet,
  secured by Security Group rules (limiting traffic to CloudFront regional IP ranges),
  and fronted by a CloudFront distribution providing global HTTPS access and caching.

Parameters:
  VpcCidr:
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC.
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16

  PublicSubnetCidr:
    Description: CIDR block for the public subnet.
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/24

  CodeServerVersion:
    Description: Version of code-server to install.
    Type: String
    Default: "4.9.1"

  CloudFrontCidr1:
    Description: Allowed CloudFront regional CIDR range 1.
    Type: String
    Default: 13.32.0.0/15

  CloudFrontCidr2:
    Description: Allowed CloudFront regional CIDR range 2.
    Type: String
    Default: 52.46.0.0/18

  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type for the VS Code development environment.
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro

Mappings:
  UbuntuAmiMap:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c94855ba95c71c99
    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-0b0af3577fe5e3532
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-02a1b208c5d2b8f0f
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-09e67e426f25ce0d7

Resources:

  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCidr
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodeVPC

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PublicSubnetCidr
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodePublicSubnet

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodeIGW

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodePublicRT

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  VsCodeSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security Group for VS Code EC2 instance allowing inbound traffic on port 8080 from CloudFront IP ranges.
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodeSG

  VsCodeIngressFromCloudFront1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress
    Properties:
      GroupId: !Ref VsCodeSecurityGroup
      IpProtocol: tcp
      FromPort: 8080
      ToPort: 8080
      CidrIp: !Ref CloudFrontCidr1
      Description: Ingress from CloudFront CIDR 1

  VsCodeIngressFromCloudFront2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress
    Properties:
      GroupId: !Ref VsCodeSecurityGroup
      IpProtocol: tcp
      FromPort: 8080
      ToPort: 8080
      CidrIp: !Ref CloudFrontCidr2
      Description: Ingress from CloudFront CIDR 2

  VsCodeInstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodeInstanceRole

  VsCodeInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref VsCodeInstanceRole
      Path: /

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: !FindInMap [UbuntuAmiMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", AMI]
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref VsCodeSecurityGroup
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref VsCodeInstanceProfile
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: VsCodeEC2Instance
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash
          set -ex
          # Update and install prerequisites
          apt-get update -y
          apt-get install -y curl jq
          # Install code-server
          CODE_SERVER_VERSION="${CodeServerVersion}"
          curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh -s -- --version ${CodeServerVersion}
          # Create a systemd service for code-server
          cat << 'EOF' > /etc/systemd/system/code-server.service
          [Unit]
          Description=code-server
          After=network.target
          
          [Service]
          Type=simple
          Environment=PASSWORD=
          ExecStart=/usr/bin/code-server --bind-addr 0.0.0.0:8080 --auth none
          Restart=always
          
          [Install]
          WantedBy=multi-user.target
          EOF
          # Reload systemd daemon and start code-server
          systemctl daemon-reload
          systemctl enable code-server
          systemctl start code-server

  VsCodeCachePolicy:
    Type: AWS::CloudFront::CachePolicy
    Properties:
      CachePolicyConfig:
        Name: VsCodeCachePolicy
        DefaultTTL: 31536000
        MinTTL: 31536000
        MaxTTL: 31536000
        ParametersInCacheKeyAndForwardedToOrigin:
          EnableAcceptEncodingGzip: true
          CookiesConfig:
            CookieBehavior: none
          HeadersConfig:
            HeaderBehavior: none
          QueryStringsConfig:
            QueryStringBehavior: all

  VsCodeCloudFrontDistribution:
    Type: AWS::CloudFront::Distribution
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
        Enabled: true
        Origins:
          - Id: VsCodeOrigin
            DomainName: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicDnsName
            CustomOriginConfig:
              HTTPPort: 8080
              HTTPSPort: 443
              OriginProtocolPolicy: http-only
        DefaultCacheBehavior:
          TargetOriginId: VsCodeOrigin
          ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
          AllowedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
            - PUT
            - POST
            - PATCH
            - DELETE
          CachePolicyId: !Ref VsCodeCachePolicy
        ViewerCertificate:
          CloudFrontDefaultCertificate: true

Outputs:
  CloudFrontURL:
    Description: "URL of the CloudFront Distribution"
    Value: !Sub "https://${VsCodeCloudFrontDistribution.DomainName}/"
  InstanceId:
    Description: "EC2 Instance ID"
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  InstancePublicIP:
    Description: "EC2 Instance Public IP"
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp
  InstancePublicDNS:
    Description: "EC2 Instance Public DNS"
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicDnsName

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an Amazon ECS Cluster with Fargate and FARGATE_SPOT capacity providers.
  
Parameters:
  ECSClusterNameSuffix:
    Description: A unique suffix for the ECS Cluster name.
    Type: String
    Default: "ECSCluster"

Resources:
  ECSCluster:
    Type: AWS::ECS::Cluster
    Properties:
      ClusterName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-${ECSClusterNameSuffix}"
      CapacityProviders:
        - FARGATE
        - FARGATE_SPOT
      DefaultCapacityProviderStrategy:
        - CapacityProvider: FARGATE_SPOT
          Weight: 1
        - CapacityProvider: FARGATE
          Weight: 1

Outputs:
  ECSClusterName:
    Description: The name of the ECS Cluster.
    Value: !Ref ECSCluster
  ECSClusterArn:

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an ECS Fargate Task Definition with a public NGINX container,
  integrated CloudWatch Logs (7-day retention), an IAM Task Role with SSM session management permissions,
  and an IAM Task Execution Role with permissions for pulling container images and writing logs.

Resources:
  NginxTaskLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: "/ecs/nginx-task"
      RetentionInDays: 7

  EcsTaskRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "ecs-task-role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SSMSessionPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssmmessages:CreateControlChannel
                  - ssmmessages:CreateDataChannel
                  - ssmmessages:OpenControlChannel
                  - ssmmessages:OpenDataChannel
                Resource: "*"

  EcsTaskExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "ecs-task-execution-role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ECSTaskExecutionPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
                  - ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability
                  - ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/ecs/nginx-task*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/ecs/nginx-task:log-stream:*"

  EcsFargateTaskDefinition:
    Type: AWS::ECS::TaskDefinition
    Properties:
      Family: !Sub "nginx-task-${AWS::StackName}"
      RequiresCompatibilities:
        - FARGATE
      Cpu: "256"
      Memory: "512"
      NetworkMode: awsvpc
      TaskRoleArn: !GetAtt EcsTaskRole.Arn
      ExecutionRoleArn: !GetAtt EcsTaskExecutionRole.Arn
      ContainerDefinitions:
        - Name: nginx
          Image: nginx:latest
          PortMappings:
            - ContainerPort: 80
              Protocol: tcp
          LogConfiguration:
            LogDriver: awslogs
            Options:
              awslogs-group: !Ref NginxTaskLogGroup
              awslogs-region: !Ref AWS::Region
              awslogs-stream-prefix: nginx

Outputs:
  EcsTaskDefinitionArn:
    Description: "ARN of the ECS Fargate Task Definition"
    Value: !Ref EcsFargateTaskDefinition

  EcsTaskRoleArn:
    Description: "ARN of the ECS Task Role with SSM permissions"
    Value: !GetAtt EcsTaskRole.Arn

  EcsTaskExecutionRoleArn:
    Description: "ARN of the ECS Task Execution Role"
    Value: !GetAtt EcsTaskExecutionRole.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that provisions a Windows Server 2019 EC2 instance with SSM agent integration,
  a custom SSM document for CPU stress testing, an IAM Role and Instance Profile for SSM access,
  and an IAM Role for FIS with permissions to trigger SSM commands, manage CloudWatch logs, and describe EC2 instances.

Parameters:
  WindowsAMIParameter:
    Type: "AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>"
    Description: "The SSM parameter that contains the Windows Server 2019 AMI ID."
    Default: "/aws/service/ami-windows-latest/Windows_Server-2019-English-Core-Base"
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Description: "EC2 Instance type for the Windows Server 2019 instance."
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.nano
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
    Default: t3.micro
  SubnetId:
    Type: String
    Description: "The Subnet ID in which to launch the EC2 instance. You must supply an existing, valid subnet ID from your account in the format 'subnet-<8 or 17 hex characters>'."
    AllowedPattern: "^subnet-(([0-9A-Fa-f]{8})|([0-9A-Fa-f]{17}))$"
    ConstraintDescription: "Must be a valid Subnet ID (e.g., subnet-1234abcd)."

Resources:
  SSMInstanceRole:
    Type: "AWS::IAM::Role"
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "SSMInstanceRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  SSMInstanceProfile:
    Type: "AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile"
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "SSMInstanceProfile-${AWS::StackName}"
      Roles:
        - !Ref SSMInstanceRole

  FISRole:
    Type: "AWS::IAM::Role"
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "FISRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - fis.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: FISPermissionsPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:SendCommand
                  - ssm:GetCommandInvocation
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:DescribeInstances
                  - ec2:DescribeInstanceStatus
                Resource: "*"

  WindowsInstance:
    Type: "AWS::EC2::Instance"
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: !Ref WindowsAMIParameter
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref SSMInstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "CPUStressWindowsInstance-${AWS::StackName}"

  CPUStressSSMDocument:
    Type: "AWS::SSM::Document"
    Properties:
      DocumentType: Command
      Name: !Sub "CPUStressDocument-${AWS::StackName}"
      Content:
        schemaVersion: "2.2"
        description: "SSM document to simulate CPU stress by spawning threads equal to logical processors for a configurable duration."
        parameters:
          Duration:
            type: "String"
            description: "(Optional) Duration for stress test in seconds."
            default: "60"
        mainSteps:
          - action: aws:runPowerShellScript
            name: cpuStressTest
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - |
                  param(
                      [int]$Duration = 60
                  )
                  $logicalProcessors = [System.Environment]::ProcessorCount
                  Write-Output "Starting CPU stress test with $logicalProcessors threads for $Duration seconds."
                  $stopTime = (Get-Date).AddSeconds($Duration)
                  $jobs = @()
                  for ($i = 0; $i -lt $logicalProcessors; $i++) {
                      $jobs += Start-Job -ScriptBlock {
                          param($endTime)
                          while ((Get-Date) -lt $endTime) {
                              [Math]::Sqrt(12345) | Out-Null
                          }
                      } -ArgumentList $stopTime
                  }
                  Write-Output "Waiting for all stress test jobs to complete..."
                  Wait-Job -Job $jobs
                  Write-Output "CPU stress test completed."

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: "The ID of the Windows EC2 instance."
    Value: !Ref WindowsInstance
  SSMDocumentName:
    Description: "The name of the custom SSM document for CPU stress testing."
    Value: !Ref CPUStressSSMDocument

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation Template that defines four IAM roles with tiered permissions for SecurityAdmins, Admins, Users, and NonUsers.
  Each role includes the AWS managed ReadOnlyAccess policy and an inline policy for granular FIS and role-passing controls.

Parameters:
  WorkshopServiceRoleArn:
    Type: String
    Description: "The ARN of the workshop-specific service role that can be passed by SecurityAdmins and Admins."
    Default: "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/WorkshopServiceRole"

Resources:
  SecurityAdminRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "SecurityAdmins-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SecurityAdminInlinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: 
                  - fis:*
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: iam:CreateServiceLinkedRole
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: iam:PassRole
                Resource: !Ref WorkshopServiceRoleArn

  AdminRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "Admins-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: AdminInlinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - fis:*
                  - iam:CreateServiceLinkedRole
                  - iam:PassRole
                Resource:
                  - "*"
                  - !Ref WorkshopServiceRoleArn
              - Effect: Deny
                Action: fis:*
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringEquals:
                    fis:Tag/Environment: "prod"

  UserRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "Users-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: UserInlinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - fis:StartExperiment
                  - fis:StopExperiment
                  - fis:ListExperiments
                  - fis:GetExperiment
                  - fis:TagResource
                  - fis:UntagResource
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Deny
                Action: fis:*
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringEquals:
                    fis:Tag/Environment: "prod"

  NonUserRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "NonUsers-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: NonUserInlinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Deny
                Action: fis:*
                Resource: "*"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an environment-specific DynamoDB table for storing supporter product data.
  For PROD, the table is provisioned with throughput, auto-scaling for write capacity, encryption (SSE), TTL,
  and backup tagging. For CODE, the table is created with PAY_PER_REQUEST billing mode, encryption (SSE),
  TTL, and backup tagging.

Parameters:
  Stage:
    Type: String
    Description: "Deployment stage (PROD for production or CODE for non-production)"
    AllowedValues:
      - PROD
      - CODE
    Default: CODE

Conditions:
  IsProd: !Equals [!Ref Stage, "PROD"]

Resources:
  SupporterProductDataTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub "SupporterProductData-${Stage}"
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: id
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: id
          KeyType: HASH
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      TimeToLiveSpecification:
        AttributeName: expiryDate
        Enabled: true
      ProvisionedThroughput: !If 
        - IsProd
        - 
          ReadCapacityUnits: 20
          WriteCapacityUnits: 5
        - !Ref "AWS::NoValue"
      BillingMode: !If
        - IsProd
        - !Ref "AWS::NoValue"
        - PAY_PER_REQUEST
      Tags:
        - Key: Backup
          Value: "true"

  DynamoDBScalingRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Condition: IsProd
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonDynamoDBAutoscaleRole
      RoleName: !Sub "DDBAutoscaleRole-${Stage}"

  AutoScalingWriteCapacityScalableTarget:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
    Condition: IsProd
    Properties:
      MaxCapacity: 500
      MinCapacity: 5
      ResourceId: !Sub "table/SupporterProductData-${Stage}"
      RoleARN: !GetAtt DynamoDBScalingRole.Arn
      ScalableDimension: dynamodb:table:WriteCapacityUnits
      ServiceNamespace: dynamodb

  AutoScalingWriteCapacityScalingPolicy:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
    Condition: IsProd
    Properties:
      PolicyName: !Sub "DDBWriteCapacityScalingPolicy-${Stage}"
      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
      ScalingTargetId: !Ref AutoScalingWriteCapacityScalableTarget
      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
        TargetValue: 70.0
        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
          PredefinedMetricType: DynamoDBWriteCapacityUtilization
        ScaleInCooldown: 60
        ScaleOutCooldown: 60

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create three IAM Managed Policies:
    1. iOSAppS3AccessPolicy: Grants an iOS application read/write access to the S3 bucket ios-apps.sagebridge.org.
    2. DynamoDBProtectionPolicy: Denies deletion and schema modification actions in DynamoDB.
    3. RDSProtectionPolicy: Denies destructive RDS operations to protect databases and backups.

Resources:
  iOSAppS3AccessPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy
    Properties:
      ManagedPolicyName: iOSAppS3AccessPolicy
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - s3:ListBucket
              - s3:GetBucketLocation
            Resource: arn:aws:s3:::ios-apps.sagebridge.org
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - s3:GetObject
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:DeleteObject
            Resource: arn:aws:s3:::ios-apps.sagebridge.org/*

  DynamoDBProtectionPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy
    Properties:
      ManagedPolicyName: DynamoDBProtectionPolicy
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Deny
            Action:
              - dynamodb:DeleteTable
              - dynamodb:UpdateTable
            Resource: "*"

  RDSProtectionPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy
    Properties:
      ManagedPolicyName: RDSProtectionPolicy
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Deny
            Action:
              - rds:DeleteDBInstance

Description: "CloudFormation template that creates an AWS Resource Group using tag based filters"
Resources:
  ResourceGroup:
    Type: AWS::ResourceGroups::Group
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ResourceGroup"
      Description: "Resource Group for EC2 RDS SageMaker SSM and others selected via tag based filters"

        Type: TAG_FILTERS_1_0

              Values:
                - EC2
                - RDS
                - SageMaker
                - SSM

Outputs:
  ResourceGroupName:
    Description: "The name of the created AWS Resource Group"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an encrypted S3 bucket (ALBAccessLogsBucket) for ALB logging.
  The bucket enforces private access with block public access settings, bucket owner enforced controls,
  and a lifecycle policy to delete objects older than 90 days. A bucket policy allows AWS ELB accounts
  and the delivery.logs.amazonaws.com service to write logs with bucket-owner-full-control ACL.

Resources:
  ALBAccessLogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-alb-access-logs-bucket'
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldLogs
            Status: Enabled
            ExpirationInDays: 90
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      OwnershipControls:
        Rules:
          - ObjectOwnership: BucketOwnerEnforced
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  ALBAccessLogsBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref ALBAccessLogsBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowELBAccountWrite
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: "arn:aws:iam::127311923021:root"
            Action: s3:PutObject
            Resource: !Join 
              - ""
              - - "arn:aws:s3:::"
                - !Ref ALBAccessLogsBucket
                - "/*"
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                s3:x-amz-acl: bucket-owner-full-control
          - Sid: AllowDeliveryLogsWrite
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: delivery.logs.amazonaws.com
            Action: s3:PutObject
            Resource: !Join 
              - ""
              - - "arn:aws:s3:::"
                - !Ref ALBAccessLogsBucket
                - "/*"
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                s3:x-amz-acl: bucket-owner-full-control

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a secure and cost-optimized S3 bucket for storing
  CloudWatch export data with configurable versioning and automated lifecycle management.

Parameters:
  VersioningEnabled:
    Description: Enable or disable versioning on the S3 bucket. Valid values are 'true' or 'false'.
    Type: String
    Default: "false"
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
  LifecycleTransitionDays:
    Description: Number of days after object creation when the object should be transitioned to a cost-optimized storage class.
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
  LifecycleExpirationDays:
    Description: Number of days after object creation when the object should be expired.
    Type: Number
    Default: 365

Conditions:
  EnableVersioning: !Equals [!Ref VersioningEnabled, "true"]

Resources:
  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "cw-export-data-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: "TransitionAndExpirationRule"
            Status: "Enabled"
            Prefix: ""
            Transitions:
              - TransitionInDays: !Ref LifecycleTransitionDays
                StorageClass: GLACIER
            ExpirationInDays: !Ref LifecycleExpirationDays
      VersioningConfiguration: 
        Fn::If:
          - EnableVersioning
          - Status: "Enabled"
          - Ref: AWS::NoValue
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  S3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref S3Bucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: "DenyNonSSLRequests"
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:*"
            Resource:
              - !Sub "${S3Bucket.Arn}"
              - !Sub "${S3Bucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: "false"

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: "The name of the S3 bucket for CloudWatch export data."
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
  BucketARN:
    Description: "The ARN of the S3 bucket for CloudWatch export data."
    Value: !GetAtt S3Bucket.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an AWS Security Hub Hub with default standards enabled
  and to enable consolidated control findings via a custom resource. Note: The consolidated control findings
  feature may not be available in every region or account configuration. The custom resource is designed
  to gracefully ignore unsupported parameter errors.

Resources:
  SecurityHubHub:
    Type: AWS::SecurityHub::Hub
    Properties:
      Tags:
        Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SecurityHubHub'
      EnableDefaultStandards: true

  CustomEnableConsolidatedFindingsRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SecurityHubUpdatePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - securityhub:UpdateSecurityHubConfiguration
                Resource: "*"

  CustomEnableConsolidatedFindingsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Description: Lambda function to enable consolidated control findings in Security Hub.
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt CustomEnableConsolidatedFindingsRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib3
          import boto3

          http = urllib3.PoolManager()

          def send_response(event, context, responseStatus, responseData, physicalResourceId=None, noEcho=False):
              responseUrl = event['ResponseURL']
              responseBody = {
                  'Status': responseStatus,
                  'Reason': 'See details in CloudWatch Log Stream: ' + context.log_stream_name,
                  'PhysicalResourceId': physicalResourceId or context.log_stream_name,
                  'StackId': event['StackId'],
                  'RequestId': event['RequestId'],
                  'LogicalResourceId': event['LogicalResourceId'],
                  'NoEcho': noEcho,
                  'Data': responseData
              }
              json_responseBody = json.dumps(responseBody)
              headers = {
                  'content-type': '',
                  'content-length': str(len(json_responseBody))
              }
              try:
                  http.request('PUT', responseUrl, body=json_responseBody.encode('utf-8'), headers=headers)
              except Exception as ex:
                  print("send_response failed executing http.request: " + str(ex))

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              print("Received event: " + json.dumps(event))
              client = boto3.client('securityhub')
              try:
                  if event['RequestType'] in ['Create', 'Update']:
                      client.update_security_hub_configuration(ConsolidatedControlFindingsEnabled=True)
                  send_response(event, context, "SUCCESS", {})
              except Exception as e:
                  error_msg = str(e)
                  # Check if error indicates unsupported parameter for consolidated control findings
                  if "ConsolidatedControlFindingsEnabled" in error_msg:
                      print("Warning: ConsolidatedControlFindingsEnabled parameter not supported. Ignoring error: " + error_msg)
                      send_response(event, context, "SUCCESS", {"Warning": error_msg})
                  else:
                      print("Exception: " + error_msg)
                      send_response(event, context, "FAILED", {"Message": error_msg})

  EnableConsolidatedControlFindingsCustomResource:
    Type: Custom::EnableConsolidatedControlFindings
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt CustomEnableConsolidatedFindingsFunction.Arn
      Tags:
        Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ConsolidatedControlFindings'

Outputs:
  SecurityHubHubArn:
    Description: ARN of the Security Hub Hub.
    Value: !GetAtt SecurityHubHub.ARN

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create AWS resources for EC2 Image Builder.
  This template provisions an IAM Role with necessary permissions for Systems Manager,
  Image Builder operations and tagging EC2 instances, an Instance Profile for the role,
  and an S3 bucket (with public access disabled) for secure image build log storage.

Resources:
  ImageBuilderRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
                - imagebuilder.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InstanceOperationsPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:SendCommand
                  - ssm:ListCommands
                  - ssm:DescribeInstanceInformation
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - imagebuilder:CreateImage
                  - imagebuilder:GetImage
                  - imagebuilder:CreateImagePipeline
                  - imagebuilder:GetImagePipeline
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:CreateTags
                Resource: "arn:aws:ec2:*:*:instance/*"
        - PolicyName: S3LogsAccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !Sub
                    - "arn:aws:s3:::${LogBucket}"
                    - {LogBucket: !Ref LogsBucket}
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                Resource:
                  - !Sub
                    - "arn:aws:s3:::${LogBucket}/*"
                    - {LogBucket: !Ref LogsBucket}

  ImageBuilderInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ImageBuilderInstanceProfile"
      Roles:
        - !Ref ImageBuilderRole

  LogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-logs-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: OwnerEmail
          Value: "u12345678@anu.edu.au"

Outputs:
  IAMRole:
    Description: "IAM Role for EC2 Image Builder operations with SSM and Image Builder permissions."
    Value: !Ref ImageBuilderRole
  InstanceProfile:
    Description: "Instance Profile for EC2 Image Builder deployments."
    Value: !Ref ImageBuilderInstanceProfile
  LogsBucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 bucket used for storing image build logs."
    Value: !Ref LogsBucket

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an S3 bucket for VPC flow logs with AES256 encryption,
  parameter-driven versioning, automated lifecycle management, and a strict bucket policy
  that only permits delivery.logs.amazonaws.com to write logs with bucket-owner-full-control ACL.

Parameters:
  S3BucketName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Optional custom name for the S3 bucket. Leave empty to auto-generate a unique name."
    Default: ""
  BucketVersioningStatus:
    Type: String
    Description: "Toggle S3 bucket versioning. Allowed values: Enabled or Suspended."
    AllowedValues:
      - Enabled
      - Suspended
    Default: Enabled
  TransitionDays:
    Type: Number
    Description: "Number of days after object creation when logs are transitioned to Glacier."
    Default: 30
  ExpirationDays:
    Type: Number
    Description: "Number of days after object creation when logs expire and are permanently deleted."
    Default: 365000

Conditions:
  UseCustomName: !Not [!Equals [!Ref S3BucketName, ""]]

Resources:
  VPCFlowLogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: !Ref BucketVersioningStatus
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: VPCFlowLogsLifecycleRule
            Status: Enabled
            Transitions:
              - TransitionInDays: !Ref TransitionDays
                StorageClass: GLACIER
            ExpirationInDays: !Ref ExpirationDays
      BucketName: !If [UseCustomName, !Ref S3BucketName, !Ref "AWS::NoValue"]

  VPCFlowLogsBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref VPCFlowLogsBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowVPCTrafficLogsDelivery
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: delivery.logs.amazonaws.com
            Action: s3:PutObject
            Resource: !Sub "${VPCFlowLogsBucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              StringEquals:

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that provisions a self-configuring Amazon Linux 2 EC2 instance.
  It installs and starts Nginx via an SSM Association using AWS-RunShellScript,
  and configures centralized logging to a dedicated S3 bucket with structured "logs/" paths.

Mappings:
  RegionSubnetMap:
    us-east-1:
      SubnetId: subnet-0abc1234
    us-west-2:
      SubnetId: subnet-0def5678
    eu-west-1:
      SubnetId: subnet-0fedcba9

Parameters:
  LatestAmiId:
    Type: 'AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>'
    Description: Latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI ID from AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store.
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2

Resources:
  SSMLogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-${AWS::AccountId}-ssm-logs"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ssm-logs"

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-InstanceRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: S3AccessForSSMLogging
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${SSMLogsBucket}"
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${SSMLogsBucket}/logs/*"

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-InstanceProfile"
      Path: /
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !FindInMap [RegionSubnetMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", SubnetId]
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: Self-Configuring-Nginx-Instance

  NginxSSMAssociation:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Association
    Properties:
      Name: AWS-RunShellScript
      Targets:
        - Key: InstanceIds
          Values:
            - !Ref EC2Instance
      Parameters:
        commands:
          - "sudo yum install -y nginx"
          - "sudo systemctl start nginx"
          - "sudo systemctl status nginx"
      OutputLocation:
        S3Location:
          OutputS3BucketName: !Ref SSMLogsBucket
          OutputS3KeyPrefix: !Sub "logs/${AWS::StackName}/ec2-ssm-output"

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: EC2 Instance ID
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  S3BucketName:
    Description: S3 Bucket for centralized SSM logs
    Value: !Ref SSMLogsBucket
  InstanceRole:
    Description: IAM Role used by the EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref InstanceRole

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy an auto-configuring Amazon Linux 2 EC2 instance with tag-driven Nginx installation,
  enhanced monitoring via CloudWatch and SSM, and centralized logging to an encrypted S3 bucket. The EC2 instance dynamically
  retrieves the latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI from AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store and configures itself via an SSM Association.

Parameters:
  LatestAmiId:
    Description: "Latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI ID (retrieved from SSM Parameter Store)."
    Type: "AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>"
    Default: "/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs"
  InstanceType:
    Description: "EC2 instance type"
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
  KeyName:
    Description: "Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance. Supply a valid KeyPair from your account."
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    ConstraintDescription: "must be the name of an existing EC2 KeyPair."
  SubnetId:
    Description: "The Subnet ID in which to launch the EC2 instance. Supply a valid SubnetId from your account."
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id

Resources:
  LogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: S3LogsAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                  - s3:DeleteObject
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "${LogsBucket.Arn}"
                  - !Sub "${LogsBucket.Arn}/*"

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      Monitoring: true
      Tags:
        - Key: "Name"
          Value: "AutoConfiguredEC2"
        - Key: "NginxInstall"
          Value: "true"
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: |
          #!/bin/bash
          # Install and configure the CloudWatch Agent for enhanced monitoring and log collection
          yum install -y amazon-cloudwatch-agent
          cat <<'EOF' > /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json
          {
            "agent": {
              "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
              "run_as_user": "root"
            },
            "metrics": {
              "append_dimensions": {
                "InstanceId": "${!aws:InstanceId}"
              },
              "metrics_collected": {
                "cpu": {
                  "measurement": [
                    "usage_active"
                  ],
                  "metrics_collection_interval": 60
                },
                "disk": {
                  "measurement": [
                    "used_percent"
                  ],
                  "metrics_collection_interval": 60,
                  "resources": [
                    "*"
                  ]
                }
              }
            },
            "logs": {
              "logs_collected": {
                "files": {
                  "collect_list": [
                    {
                      "file_path": "/var/log/nginx/access.log",
                      "log_group_name": "NginxAccessLogs",
                      "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}",
                      "timestamp_format": "%b %d %H:%M:%S"
                    },
                    {
                      "file_path": "/var/log/nginx/error.log",
                      "log_group_name": "NginxErrorLogs",
                      "log_stream_name": "{instance_id}",
                      "timestamp_format": "%b %d %H:%M:%S"
                    }
                  ]
                }
              }
            }
          }
          EOF
          /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a fetch-config -m ec2 -c file:/opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json -s

  NginxInstallAssociation:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Association
    Properties:
      Name: AWS-RunShellScript
      AssociationName: "NginxInstallAssociation"
      Targets:
        - Key: "tag:NginxInstall"
          Values:
            - "true"
      Parameters:
        commands:
          - "sudo amazon-linux-extras install nginx1 -y"
          - "sudo systemctl start nginx"
          - "sudo systemctl enable nginx"
      DocumentVersion: "$LATEST"

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: "Instance Id of the deployed EC2 instance"
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  LogsBucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 Bucket that stores the centralized logs"
    Value: !Ref LogsBucket

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create an EC2 instance running Amazon Linux 2,
  with automated OS updates, Nginx installation, and validation workflows
  orchestrated via an SSM Automation Document. The template also provisions
  an S3 bucket for centralized logging and enforces least-privilege IAM policies.

Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Description: (Optional) EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance.
    Type: String
    Default: ""
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type.
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.nano
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
    ConstraintDescription: must be a valid EC2 instance type.
  SubnetId:
    Description: "Subnet ID in which to launch the EC2 instance. Provide a valid subnet from your environment."
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
  SecurityGroupIds:
    Description: "Security Group IDs for the EC2 instance (comma-delimited list). Provide valid security groups from your environment."
    Type: List<AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup::Id>

Resources:
  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: EC2SSMInstanceRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: S3LoggingAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource: !Sub arn:aws:s3:::${LoggingBucket}/*

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: EC2InstanceProfile
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !If
        - HasKeyName
        - !Ref KeyName
        - !Ref "AWS::NoValue"
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs}}"
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroupIds: !Ref SecurityGroupIds
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: |
          #!/bin/bash
          # Enable automatic OS updates and reboot if necessary
          sudo yum update -y
          # Check if a reboot is required (this file is created by some update packages)
          if [ -f /var/run/reboot-required ]; then
            sudo shutdown -r now
          fi
          # Install and start CloudWatch Agent (assumes configuration is delivered by SSM or pre-baked)
          sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a start

  LoggingBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-logs-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled

  LoggingBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref LoggingBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowInstanceSSMLoggingAccess
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !GetAtt EC2InstanceRole.Arn
            Action:
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:GetObject
            Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${LoggingBucket}/*"
          - Sid: DenyUnEncryptedObjectUploads
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:PutObject"
            Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${LoggingBucket}/*"
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256

  SSMAutomationDocument:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Document
    Properties:
      DocumentType: Automation
      DocumentFormat: YAML
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EC2ConfigurationAutomation"
      Content:
        schemaVersion: '0.3'
        description: >
          Automation document to perform OS updates with reboot check,
          install Nginx, and validate the web server installation using curl.
        assumeRole: "{{ AutomationAssumeRole }}"
        parameters: {}
        mainSteps:
          - name: OSUpdate
            action: aws:runCommand
            inputs:
              DocumentName: AWS-RunShellScript
              Parameters:
                commands:
                  - "sudo yum update -y"
                  - |
                    if [ -f /var/run/reboot-required ]; then
                      echo 'Reboot required. Rebooting now.'
                      sudo shutdown -r now
                    fi
          - name: InstallNginx
            action: aws:runCommand
            inputs:
              DocumentName: AWS-RunShellScript
              Parameters:
                commands:
                  - "sudo amazon-linux-extras install nginx1.12 -y"
                  - "sudo systemctl start nginx"
                  - "sudo systemctl enable nginx"
          - name: ValidateNginx
            action: aws:runCommand
            inputs:
              DocumentName: AWS-RunShellScript
              Parameters:
                commands:
                  - "curl -f http://localhost || { echo 'Nginx validation failed'; exit 1; }"

Conditions:
  HasKeyName: !Not [!Equals [!Ref KeyName, ""]]

Outputs:
  EC2InstanceID:
    Description: "ID of the launched EC2 instance"
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  SSMAutomationDocumentName:
    Description: "Name of the SSM Automation Document"
    Value: !Ref SSMAutomationDocument
  LoggingBucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 bucket for centralized logging"
    Value: !Ref LoggingBucket

    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-vpc'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-public-subnet'

  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-private-subnet-1'

  PrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.3.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [2, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-private-subnet-2'

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-igw'

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-public-rt'

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  NatEIP:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIP
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      Domain: vpc

  NatGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NatGateway
    DependsOn: PublicSubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      AllocationId: !GetAtt NatEIP.AllocationId
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-natgw'

  PrivateRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-private-rt'

  PrivateRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      NatGatewayId: !Ref NatGateway

  PrivateSubnet1RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  PrivateSubnet2RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  ApplicationSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      GroupDescription: Security group allowing HTTP, HTTPS, SSH, and PostgreSQL traffic
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 5432
          ToPort: 5432
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-app-sg'

  InternalALB:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${Environment}-internal-alb'
      Scheme: internal
      Subnets:
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet1
        - !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref ApplicationSecurityGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment

  ALBListener:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref InternalALB
      Port: 80
      Protocol: HTTP
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: fixed-response
          FixedResponseConfig:
            ContentType: text/plain
            MessageBody: "Service unavailable"
            StatusCode: "503"

  ECRRepository:
    Type: AWS::ECR::Repository
    Properties:
      RepositoryName: !Sub '${Environment}-app-repo'
      ImageScanningConfiguration:
        ScanOnPush: true
      LifecyclePolicy:
        LifecyclePolicyText: |
          {
            "rules": [
              {
                "rulePriority": 10,
                "description": "Expire untagged images older than 14 days",
                "selection": {
                  "tagStatus": "untagged",
                  "countType": "sinceImagePushed",
                  "countUnit": "days",
                  "countNumber": 14
                },
                "action": {
                  "type": "expire"
                }
              }
            ]
          }

  SSMParameterVPCID:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/vpc/vpc-id'
      Description: VPC ID
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref VPC
      Tier: Standard

  SSMParameterPublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/vpc/public-subnet-id'
      Description: Public Subnet ID
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref PublicSubnet
      Tier: Standard

  SSMParameterPrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/vpc/private-subnet-1-id'
      Description: Private Subnet 1 ID
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
      Tier: Standard

  SSMParameterPrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/vpc/private-subnet-2-id'
      Description: Private Subnet 2 ID
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      Tier: Standard

  SSMParameterSecurityGroupID:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/vpc/security-group-id'
      Description: Application Security Group ID
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref ApplicationSecurityGroup
      Tier: Standard

  SSMParameterALBListenerARN:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '/${Environment}/alb/listener-arn'
      Description: ALB Listener ARN
      Type: String
      Value: !Ref ALBListener
      Tier: Standard

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: VPC Identifier
    Value: !Ref VPC
  PublicSubnetId:
    Description: Public Subnet Identifier
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet
  PrivateSubnet1Id:
    Description: Private Subnet 1 Identifier
    Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
  PrivateSubnet2Id:
    Description: Private Subnet 2 Identifier
    Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
  ApplicationSecurityGroupId:
    Description: Application Security Group Identifier
    Value: !Ref ApplicationSecurityGroup
  ALBListenerARN:
    Description: ALB Listener ARN
    Value: !Ref ALBListener
  ECRRepositoryName:
    Description: ECR Repository Name
    Value: !Ref ECRRepository

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that creates a serverless pipeline for collecting,
  storing, and analyzing AWS Organization data for centralized governance and reporting.
Parameters:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: "The name of the S3 bucket to store organizational data."
    Type: String
    Default: "org-data-bucket-default"
  ReportTags:
    Description: "Comma-separated list of tags to include in reports."
    Type: String
    Default: "Environment,Owner"
  ManagementAccountID:
    Description: "AWS Management Account ID for cross-account role assumption."
    Type: String
    Default: "123456789012"
  AthenaDatabaseName:
    Description: "The Athena database name to catalog the data (and Glue Database name)."
    Type: String
    Default: "managementcur"

Resources:
  OrganizationDataBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Ref S3BucketName
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled

  OrganizationDataBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref OrganizationDataBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyNonSSLRequests
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:*"
            Resource:
              - !Sub "${OrganizationDataBucket.Arn}"
              - !Sub "${OrganizationDataBucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: false

  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "OrgDataLambdaExecutionRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: OrgDataAccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Sid: AllowAssumeCrossAccountRole
                Effect: Allow
                Action: sts:AssumeRole
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${ManagementAccountID}:role/*"
              - Sid: OrganizationsReadAccess
                Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - organizations:ListAccounts
                  - organizations:DescribeOrganization
                  - organizations:ListOrganizationalUnitsForParent
                  - organizations:ListTagsForResource
                Resource: "*"
              - Sid: S3WriteAccess
                Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:PutObjectAcl
                Resource: !Sub "${OrganizationDataBucket.Arn}/*"

  OrgDataLambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "OrgDataCollector-${AWS::StackName}"
      Runtime: python3.12
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Environment:
        Variables:
          TARGET_BUCKET: !Ref OrganizationDataBucket
          MANAGEMENT_ACCOUNT_ID: !Ref ManagementAccountID
          REPORT_TAGS: !Ref ReportTags
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json, boto3, os, datetime
          import botocore.exceptions

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              sts_client = boto3.client('sts')
              # Assume role in management account; role ARN format: arn:aws:iam::{ManagementAccountID}:role/YourCrossAccountRole
              management_account_id = os.environ.get('MANAGEMENT_ACCOUNT_ID')
              role_arn = f"arn:aws:iam::{management_account_id}:role/YourCrossAccountRole"
              try:
                  assumed_role = sts_client.assume_role(
                      RoleArn=role_arn,
                      RoleSessionName='OrgDataSession'
                  )
              except botocore.exceptions.ClientError as e:
                  print("Error assuming role:", e)
                  return { "status": "error", "message": str(e) }
              
              credentials = assumed_role['Credentials']
              org_client = boto3.client(
                  'organizations',
                  aws_access_key_id=credentials['AccessKeyId'],
                  aws_secret_access_key=credentials['SecretAccessKey'],
                  aws_session_token=credentials['SessionToken']
              )
              try:
                  accounts = org_client.list_accounts()
                  organization = org_client.describe_organization()
                  result = {
                      "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                      "accounts": accounts.get('Accounts', []),
                      "organization": organization.get('Organization', {})
                  }
              except Exception as e:
                  print("Error fetching organization data:", e)
                  result = { "status": "error", "message": str(e) }
              
              s3_client = boto3.client('s3')
              bucket = os.environ.get('TARGET_BUCKET')
              key = f"orgdata/{datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ')}.json"
              s3_client.put_object(
                  Bucket=bucket,
                  Key=key,
                  Body=json.dumps(result).encode('utf-8'),
                  ContentType='application/json'
              )
              return { "status": "success", "s3_key": key }

  WeeklyLambdaSchedule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "OrgDataLambdaWeeklySchedule-${AWS::StackName}"
      ScheduleExpression: "cron(0 0 ? * MON *)"
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt OrgDataLambdaFunction.Arn
          Id: "OrgDataLambdaTarget"

  LambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref OrgDataLambdaFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: events.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt WeeklyLambdaSchedule.Arn

  GlueServiceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "GlueServiceRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: glue.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSGlueServiceRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: GlueS3AccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Sid: AllowS3DataAccess
                Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt OrganizationDataBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${OrganizationDataBucket.Arn}/*"

  GlueDatabase:
    Type: AWS::Glue::Database
    Properties:
      CatalogId: !Ref AWS::AccountId
      DatabaseInput:
        Name: !Ref AthenaDatabaseName
        Description: "Glue Catalog database for AWS Organization data."

  GlueCrawler:
    Type: AWS::Glue::Crawler
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "OrgDataCrawler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Role: !GetAtt GlueServiceRole.Arn
      DatabaseName: !Ref GlueDatabase
      Targets:
        S3Targets:
          - Path: !Sub "s3://${OrganizationDataBucket}/orgdata/"
      Schedule:
        ScheduleExpression: "cron(0 3 ? * SUN *)"
      SchemaChangePolicy:
        UpdateBehavior: LOG
        DeleteBehavior: DEPRECATE_IN_DATABASE

Outputs:
  LambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "ARN of the OrgData Lambda Function."
    Value: !GetAtt OrgDataLambdaFunction.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LambdaFunctionARN"
  S3BucketARN:
    Description: "ARN of the S3 bucket storing organization data."
    Value: !GetAtt OrganizationDataBucket.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-S3BucketARN"
  GlueCrawlerARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Glue Crawler resource."
    Value: !Sub "arn:aws:glue:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:crawler/OrgDataCrawler-${AWS::StackName}"
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-GlueCrawlerARN"
  LambdaExecutionRoleARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Lambda execution IAM Role."
    Value: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LambdaExecutionRoleARN"
  GlueServiceRoleARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Glue service IAM Role."
    Value: !GetAtt GlueServiceRole.Arn
    Export:
