final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  AWS Batch event‐driven processing pipeline integrated with SQS and EventBridge Pipes.
  This template provisions a secure VPC with private subnets, an SQS queue as the event source,
  an AWS Batch compute environment, job queue and job definition, an EventBridge Pipe that filters
  messages to trigger Batch jobs, and the necessary IAM roles following least privilege.

Parameters:
  VpcCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC.
  PrivateSubnet1Cidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.1.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for private subnet 1.
  PrivateSubnet2Cidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.2.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for private subnet 2.

Resources:

  MyVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCidr
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet1Cidr
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet1"

  PrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet2Cidr
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet2"

  BatchSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for AWS Batch compute resources
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 0
          ToPort: 65535
          CidrIp: !Ref VpcCidr
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchSG"

  BatchInstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchInstanceRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonEC2ContainerServiceforEC2Role
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchInstanceRole"

  BatchInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchInstanceProfile"
      Roles:
        - !Ref BatchInstanceRole

  BatchServiceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchServiceRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: batch.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSBatchServiceRole
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchServiceRole"

  ECSExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ECSExecutionRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ECSExecutionRole"

  EventBridgePipesRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EventBridgePipesRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: pipes.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgePipesPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sqs:ReceiveMessage
                  - sqs:DeleteMessage
                  - sqs:GetQueueAttributes
                Resource: !GetAtt SQSQueue.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - batch:SubmitJob
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt BatchJobDefinition.JobDefinitionArn
                  - !GetAtt BatchJobQueue.JobQueueArn
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EventBridgePipesRole"

  SQSQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub "order-created-queue-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      KmsMasterKeyId: alias/aws/sqs
      KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds: 300
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SQSQueue"

  BatchComputeEnvironment:
    Type: AWS::Batch::ComputeEnvironment
    Properties:
      ComputeEnvironmentName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ComputeEnv"
      Type: MANAGED
      ServiceRole: !GetAtt BatchServiceRole.Arn
      ComputeResources:
        Type: EC2
        MinvCpus: 0
        MaxvCpus: 16
        DesiredvCpus: 0
        InstanceTypes:
          - m5.large
        InstanceRole: !GetAtt BatchInstanceProfile.Arn
        Subnets:
          - !Ref PrivateSubnet1
          - !Ref PrivateSubnet2
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref BatchSecurityGroup
        AllocationStrategy: BEST_FIT
        Tags:
          Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchComputeResource"

  BatchJobQueue:
    Type: AWS::Batch::JobQueue
    Properties:
      JobQueueName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-JobQueue"
      Priority: 1
      ComputeEnvironmentOrder:
        - Order: 1
          ComputeEnvironment: !Ref BatchComputeEnvironment
      Tags:
        Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-JobQueue"

  BatchJobDefinition:
    Type: AWS::Batch::JobDefinition
    Properties:
      JobDefinitionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-JobDefinition"
      Type: container
      ContainerProperties:
        Image: amazonlinux
        Vcpus: 1
        Memory: 1024
        Command: []
        ExecutionRoleArn: !GetAtt ECSExecutionRole.Arn
      Tags:
        Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-JobDefinition"

  EventBridgePipe:
    Type: AWS::Pipes::Pipe
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-Pipe"
      RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgePipesRole.Arn
      Source: !GetAtt SQSQueue.Arn
      SourceParameters:
        SqsQueueParameters:
          BatchSize: 1
      Target: !GetAtt BatchJobQueue.JobQueueArn
      TargetParameters:
        BatchJobParameters:
          JobDefinition: !Ref BatchJobDefinition
          JobName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-OrderProcessingJob"

Outputs:

    Description: VPC ID
    Value: !Ref MyVPC
  SQSQueueURL:
    Description: SQS Queue URL
    Value: !Ref SQSQueue
  BatchComputeEnvironment:
    Description: AWS Batch Compute Environment
    Value: !Ref BatchComputeEnvironment
  BatchJobQueue:
    Description: AWS Batch Job Queue
    Value: !Ref BatchJobQueue
  BatchJobDefinition:
    Description: AWS Batch Job Definition
    Value: !Ref BatchJobDefinition
  EventBridgePipeName:
    Description: Name of the EventBridge Pipe
    Value: !Ref EventBridgePipe

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that provisions Linux and Windows EC2 instances with SSM/S3 IAM roles,
  an SSM Document with platform‐specific commands, an SSM State Manager Association targeting instances with tag HelloWorld:true,
  and an EventBridge Scheduler to trigger the association every 15 minutes.

Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Description: EC2 KeyPair for SSH access (Linux) and RDP access (Windows). Provide an existing key pair.
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Default: "existing-keypair"
  VpcId:
    Description: VPC Id to launch EC2 instances. Provide an existing VPC ID.
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Default: "vpc-12345678"
  SubnetId:
    Description: Subnet Id to launch EC2 instances. Provide an existing Subnet ID.
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Default: "subnet-12345678"

Resources:

  ##############################
  # IAM Role and Instance Profile for EC2 Instances
  ##############################
  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EC2InstanceRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SSMAndS3AccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:SendCommand
                  - ssm:UpdateInstanceInformation
                  - ssm:DescribeInstanceInformation
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::execution-logs-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::StackName}"
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::execution-logs-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::StackName}/*"

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "EC2InstanceProfile-${AWS::StackName}"
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  ##############################
  # IAM Role for EventBridge Scheduler to invoke SSM actions
  ##############################
  EventBridgeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "EventBridgeSchedulerRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: scheduler.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SSMInvokePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:StartAssociationsOnce
                Resource: "*"

  ##############################
  # Security Group for EC2 Instances
  ##############################
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security Group for EC2 instances with SSM access.
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 3389
          ToPort: 3389
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  ##############################
  # Linux EC2 Instance
  ##############################
  LinuxEC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2}}"
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: "0"
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: "true"
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "LinuxInstance-${AWS::StackName}"

  ##############################
  # Windows EC2 Instance
  ##############################
  WindowsEC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-windows-latest/Windows_Server-2019-English-Full-Base}}"
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: "0"
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      Tags:
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: "true"
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "WindowsInstance-${AWS::StackName}"

  ##############################
  # SSM Document with platform-specific commands
  ##############################
  HelloWorldSSMDocument:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Document
    Properties:
      DocumentType: Command
      Name: !Sub "HelloWorldDocument-${AWS::StackName}"
      Content:
        schemaVersion: "2.2"
        description: "Echo Hello World on both Linux and Windows instances"
        mainSteps:
          - action: "aws:runShellScript"
            name: "RunShellScript"
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - "echo 'Hello World!'"
          - action: "aws:runPowerShellScript"
            name: "RunPowerShellScript"
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - "Write-Output 'Hello World!'"

  ##############################
  # SSM State Manager Association targeting instances with tag HelloWorld:true
  ##############################
  SSMAssociation:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Association
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref HelloWorldSSMDocument
      AssociationName: !Sub "HelloWorldAssociation-${AWS::StackName}"
      Targets:
        - Key: "tag:HelloWorld"
          Values:
            - "true"

  ##############################
  # EventBridge Scheduler that triggers the SSM Association every 15 minutes
  ##############################
  SsmAssociationScheduler:
    Type: AWS::Scheduler::Schedule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "SsmAssociationScheduler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Description: "Triggers SSM Association (StartAssociationsOnce) every 15 minutes"
      ScheduleExpression: "rate(15 minutes)"
      FlexibleTimeWindow:
        Mode: "OFF"
      Target:
        RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeRole.Arn
        Arn: !Sub "arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:association/${SSMAssociation}"
        Input: !Sub '{"AssociationIds": ["${SSMAssociation}"]}'

  ##############################
  # S3 Bucket for execution logs with encryption and public access restrictions
  ##############################
  LogBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "execution-logs-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::StackName}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  LogBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref LogBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyPublicAccess
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:*"
            Resource:
              - !Sub "${LogBucket.Arn}"
              - !Sub "${LogBucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: false

Outputs:
  LinuxInstanceId:
    Description: "EC2 Instance ID for the Linux instance"
    Value: !Ref LinuxEC2Instance
  WindowsInstanceId:
    Description: "EC2 Instance ID for the Windows instance"
    Value: !Ref WindowsEC2Instance
  LogBucketName:
    Description: "S3 Bucket name for execution logs"
    Value: !Ref LogBucket
  SsmScheduleArn:
    Description: "EventBridge Scheduler ARN triggering the SSM Association"
    Value: !GetAtt SsmAssociationScheduler.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to integrate AWS EventBridge with Shopify's product creation API.
  This template provisions an EventBridge event bus, a connection using a Shopify admin API key,
  an API destination pointing to the Shopify product creation endpoint, and a rule that triggers
  on events from source "MyShopifyApp" to automate product updates in Shopify.

Parameters:
  ShopifyAdminAPIKey:
    Type: String
    Description: "The Shopify admin API key used for authentication with Shopify."
    NoEcho: true
    Default: "YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"

Resources:
  ShopifyEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: ShopifyIntegrationEventBus
      Tags:
        - Key: Integration
          Value: Shopify

  ShopifyConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: ShopifyConnection
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: 'X-Shopify-Access-Token'
          ApiKeyValue: !Ref ShopifyAdminAPIKey

  ShopifyAPIDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: ShopifyAPIDestination
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt ShopifyConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: 'https://testanu.myshopify.com/admin/api/products.json'
      HttpMethod: POST
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10

  EventBridgeInvokeAPIDestinationRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeAPIDestinationPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource: !GetAtt ShopifyAPIDestination.Arn

  ShopifyEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: ShopifyProductCreationRule
      EventBusName: !Ref ShopifyEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "MyShopifyApp"
      Targets:
        - Id: ShopifyTarget
          Arn: !GetAtt ShopifyAPIDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeInvokeAPIDestinationRole.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template for integrating Amazon EventBridge with Stripe’s Product API.
  This template provisions an EventBridge event bus to capture PartnerApp events, a Connection
  resource with API key authentication, an API Destination targeting Stripe's products endpoint,
  and an EventBridge rule to route events accordingly.
  NOTE: For development purposes, the Stripe API key is provided as a plain text parameter.
  In production, supply a secure API key (for example, via AWS Secrets Manager or a pre-created SSM SecureString parameter)
  and ensure that the executing IAM principal has ssm:GetParameter or SecretsManager:GetSecretValue permissions as appropriate.

Parameters:
  StripeApiKey:
    Type: String
    Default: "PLACEHOLDER_STRIPE_API_KEY"
    Description: "The Stripe API key. For development, this is provided in plain text. In production, supply a secure API key."

Resources:
  PartnerAppEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PartnerAppBus"
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: StripeIntegration

  StripeApiConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-StripeApiConnection"
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: Authorization
          ApiKeyValue: !Ref StripeApiKey

  StripeApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-StripeProductDestination"
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt StripeApiConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: "https://api.stripe.com/v1/products"
      HttpMethod: POST
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10

  EventBridgeInvokeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EventBridgeInvokeRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeApiDestinationPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource: !GetAtt StripeApiDestination.Arn

  PartnerAppRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PartnerAppProductCreationRule"
      EventBusName: !Ref PartnerAppEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "PartnerApp"
        detail-type:
          - "PartnerAppDetailType"
      Targets:
        - Id: "StripeApiDestinationTarget"
          Arn: !GetAtt StripeApiDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeInvokeRole.Arn

Outputs:
  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: "The ARN of the API Destination targeting Stripe's product endpoint."
    Value: !GetAtt StripeApiDestination.Arn
  ConnectionArn:
    Description: "The ARN of the EventBridge Connection for the Stripe API integration."
    Value: !GetAtt StripeApiConnection.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation Template that creates a secure REST API Gateway integrated with
  Amazon DynamoDB for managing music data. It provisions a DynamoDB table called "Music"
  with a global secondary index "Artist-Index", an API Gateway with POST and GET methods
  for adding and retrieving music entries, API key authentication, an IAM role for API Gateway
  to access DynamoDB, and a usage plan with rate limiting/throttling.

Resources:
  MusicTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: Music
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: id
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: artist
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: id
          KeyType: HASH
      GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: Artist-Index
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: artist
              KeyType: HASH
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL
          ProvisionedThroughput:
            ReadCapacityUnits: 5
            WriteCapacityUnits: 5
      ProvisionedThroughput:
        ReadCapacityUnits: 5
        WriteCapacityUnits: 5

  ApiGatewayDynamoDBRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: apigateway.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ApiGatewayDynamoDBPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - dynamodb:PutItem
                  - dynamodb:Query
                Resource: !GetAtt MusicTable.Arn

  MusicAPI:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: MusicAPI

  MusicResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      ParentId: !GetAtt MusicAPI.RootResourceId
      PathPart: music
      RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI

  PostMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI
      ResourceId: !Ref MusicResource
      HttpMethod: POST
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      ApiKeyRequired: true
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:dynamodb:action/PutItem"
        Credentials: !GetAtt ApiGatewayDynamoDBRole.Arn
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json: |
            {
              "TableName": "Music",
              "Item": {
                "id": { "S": "$context.requestId" },
                "artist": { "S": "$input.path('$.artist')" },
                "album": { "S": "$input.path('$.album')" }
              }
            }
        IntegrationResponses:
          - StatusCode: 200
            ResponseTemplates:
              application/json: ""
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200

  GetMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI
      ResourceId: !Ref MusicResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      ApiKeyRequired: true
      RequestParameters:
        method.request.querystring.artist: true
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:dynamodb:action/Query"
        Credentials: !GetAtt ApiGatewayDynamoDBRole.Arn
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json: |
            {
              "TableName": "Music",
              "IndexName": "Artist-Index",
              "KeyConditionExpression": "artist = :v_artist",
              "ExpressionAttributeValues": {
                ":v_artist": { "S": "$input.params('artist')" }
              }
            }
        IntegrationResponses:
          - StatusCode: 200
            ResponseTemplates:
              application/json: ""
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200

  ApiDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn:
      - PostMethod
      - GetMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI

  MusicApiStage:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Stage
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI
      DeploymentId: !Ref ApiDeployment
      Description: "Production stage for Music API"

  ApiUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: MusicAPIUsagePlan
      Description: "Usage plan for Music API with rate limiting and throttling"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref MusicAPI
          Stage: !Ref MusicApiStage
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 100
        RateLimit: 50

  ApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    Properties:
      Name: MusicAPIKey
      Enabled: true
      StageKeys:
        - RestApiId: !Ref MusicAPI
          StageName: !Ref MusicApiStage

  UsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref ApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref ApiUsagePlan

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: "API Gateway endpoint URL"
    Value: !Sub "https://${MusicAPI}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/music"
    Export:
      Name: ApiEndpoint
  ApiKeyOutput:
    Description: "API Key for accessing the Music API"
    Value: !Ref ApiKey
    Export:
      Name: ApiKey
  DynamoDBTableName:
    Description: "DynamoDB table name"
    Value: !Ref MusicTable
    Export:
      Name: DynamoDBTableName

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Secure REST API Gateway integrated with Amazon SES via a Lambda proxy integration.
  The API enforces JSON schema validation, API key authentication, rate limiting, and monthly quotas.

Parameters:
  ApiKeyValue:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Description: API Key value for accessing the API Gateway
    Default: ReplaceWithSecureKey

Resources:
  RestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: SecureSESSenderAPI
      Description: REST API integrated with SES for sending templated emails.
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - REGIONAL

  SendEmailResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      ParentId: !GetAtt RestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: sendemail
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi

  EmailRequestValidator:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RequestValidator
    Properties:
      Name: EmailRequestValidator
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ValidateRequestBody: true
      ValidateRequestParameters: false

  EmailSchemaModel:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Model
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ContentType: application/json
      Name: EmailModel
      Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          to:
            type: string
            format: email
          from:
            type: string
            format: email
          subject:
            type: string
          text:
            type: string
          html:
            type: string
        required:
          - to
          - from
          - subject
          - text
          - html

  SESIntegrationRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "ApiGateway_SES_Invoke_Role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: apigateway.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SESInvokePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ses:SendEmail
                Resource: "*"

  SendEmailLambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "SendEmailLambdaExecutionRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaBasicAndSESPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: arn:aws:logs:*:*:*
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ses:SendEmail
                Resource: "*"

  SendEmailFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "SendEmailFunction-${AWS::StackName}"
      Runtime: nodejs24.x
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt SendEmailLambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          'use strict';
          const AWS = require("aws-sdk");
          const ses = new AWS.SES();
          exports.handler = async (event) => {
              const body = event;
              // Expecting event to have keys: to, from, subject, text, html
              const params = {
                  Source: body.from,
                  Destination: {
                      ToAddresses: [body.to]
                  },
                  Message: {
                      Subject: {
                          Data: body.subject
                      },
                      Body: {
                          Text: {
                              Data: body.text
                          },
                          Html: {
                              Data: body.html
                          }
                      }
                  }
              };
              try {
                  await ses.sendEmail(params).promise();
                  return {
                      statusCode: 200,
                      body: JSON.stringify({ message: "Email request submitted successfully."})
                  };
              } catch (error) {
                  console.error(error);
                  return {
                      statusCode: 500,
                      body: JSON.stringify({ error: "Failed to send email."})
                  };
              }
          };

  SendEmailMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref SendEmailResource
      HttpMethod: POST
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      ApiKeyRequired: true
      RequestValidatorId: !Ref EmailRequestValidator
      RequestModels:
        application/json: !Ref EmailSchemaModel
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${SendEmailFunction.Arn}/invocations"
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200
          ResponseModels:
            application/json: Empty

  LambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref SendEmailFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${RestApi}/*/POST/sendemail"

  ApiDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn:
      - SendEmailMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi

  ApiStage:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Stage
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      Description: Production Stage
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      DeploymentId: !Ref ApiDeployment
      MethodSettings:
        - DataTraceEnabled: false
          HttpMethod: "*"
          LoggingLevel: INFO
          ResourcePath: "/*"

  ApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    Properties:
      Name: SecureEmailApiKey
      Enabled: true
      Value: !Ref ApiKeyValue

  UsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: SecureEmailUsagePlan
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref RestApi
          Stage: !Ref ApiStage
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 500
        RateLimit: 500
      Quota:
        Limit: 10000
        Period: MONTH

  UsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref ApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref UsagePlan

Outputs:
  RestApiId:
    Description: ID of the API Gateway REST API
    Value: !Ref RestApi
  ApiKeyId:
    Description: API Key for accessing the API
    Value: !Ref ApiKey
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: URL of the deployed API endpoint
    Value: !Sub "https://${RestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/sendemail"

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that creates a Kinesis Data Stream and an API Gateway
  REST API integrated with Kinesis Data Streams. The API provides endpoints to put single
  records, put batch records, get records via shard iterators, and retrieve stream metadata.
  An IAM Role is created to grant API Gateway permission to invoke the necessary Kinesis actions.

Resources:
  KinesisStream:
    Type: AWS::Kinesis::Stream
    Properties:
      ShardCount: 1
      RetentionPeriodHours: 24

  APIGatewayKinesisRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: apigateway.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: KinesisAccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kinesis:PutRecord
                  - kinesis:PutRecords
                  - kinesis:GetRecords
                  - kinesis:DescribeStream
                Resource: !GetAtt KinesisStream.Arn

  RestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: KinesisIntegrationApi
      Description: API Gateway integrated with Kinesis Data Streams

  KinesisResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !GetAtt RestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: kinesis

  PutRecordResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !Ref KinesisResource
      PathPart: putrecord

  PutRecordMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref PutRecordResource
      HttpMethod: PUT
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:kinesis:action/PutRecord"
        Credentials: !GetAtt APIGatewayKinesisRole.Arn
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json:
            !Sub
            - | 
              {
                "StreamName": "${StreamName}",
                "Data": "$util.base64Encode($input.json('$.data'))",
                "PartitionKey": "$input.json('$.partitionKey')"
              }
            - {StreamName: !Ref KinesisStream}
        PassthroughBehavior: NEVER
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: "200"

  PutRecordsResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !Ref KinesisResource
      PathPart: putrecords

  PutRecordsMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref PutRecordsResource
      HttpMethod: PUT
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:kinesis:action/PutRecords"
        Credentials: !GetAtt APIGatewayKinesisRole.Arn
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json:
            !Sub
            - | 
              {
                "StreamName": "${StreamName}",
                "Records": [
                  #foreach($record in $input.path('$.records'))
                    {
                      "Data": "$util.base64Encode($record.data)",
                      "PartitionKey": "$record.partitionKey"
                    }#if($foreach.hasNext),#end
                  #end
                ]
              }
            - {StreamName: !Ref KinesisStream}
        PassthroughBehavior: NEVER
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: "200"

  GetRecordsResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !Ref KinesisResource
      PathPart: getrecords

  GetRecordsMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref GetRecordsResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      RequestParameters:
        method.request.querystring.shardIterator: false
        method.request.querystring.limit: false
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:kinesis:action/GetRecords"
        Credentials: !GetAtt APIGatewayKinesisRole.Arn
        RequestParameters:
          integration.request.querystring.ShardIterator: "method.request.querystring.shardIterator"
          integration.request.querystring.Limit: "method.request.querystring.limit"
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json: |
            {
              "ShardIterator": "$input.params('shardIterator')",
              "Limit": "$input.params('limit')"
            }
        PassthroughBehavior: NEVER
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: "200"

  DescribeStreamResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ParentId: !Ref KinesisResource
      PathPart: stream

  DescribeStreamMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref DescribeStreamResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:kinesis:action/DescribeStream"
        Credentials: !GetAtt APIGatewayKinesisRole.Arn
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json:
            !Sub
            - | 
              {
                "StreamName": "${StreamName}"
              }
            - {StreamName: !Ref KinesisStream}
        PassthroughBehavior: NEVER
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: "200"

  ApiDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn:
      - PutRecordMethod
      - PutRecordsMethod
      - GetRecordsMethod
      - DescribeStreamMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref RestApi
      StageName: prod

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: "API Gateway endpoint URL (for the /kinesis path)"
    Value: !Sub "https://${RestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/kinesis"
  KinesisStreamARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Kinesis Stream"
    Value: !GetAtt KinesisStream.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  This template deploys a basic Amazon EFS file system for a testing environment.

Parameters:
  VPCId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: The VPC ID where the EFS security group will be created.
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: The Subnet ID where the EFS mount target will be deployed in us-east-1a.

Resources:
  EfsSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: !Sub "Security Group for EFS mount target in ${AWS::StackName} (allows inbound NFS traffic)"
      VpcId: !Ref VPCId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 2049
          ToPort: 2049
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EfsSecurityGroup"

  EfsFileSystem:
    Type: AWS::EFS::FileSystem
    Properties:
      PerformanceMode: generalPurpose
      Encrypted: true
      FileSystemTags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EfsFileSystem"

  EfsMountTarget:
    Type: AWS::EFS::MountTarget
    Properties:
      FileSystemId: !Ref EfsFileSystem
      SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref EfsSecurityGroup

Outputs:
  FileSystemId:
    Description: ID of the created EFS file system.
    Value: !Ref EfsFileSystem
  MountTargetId:
    Description: ID of the created EFS mount target.
    Value: !Ref EfsMountTarget

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Automated solution for managing AWS Security Hub controls.
  This template deploys a Lambda function that programmatically updates Security Hub controls
  (e.g., enabling or disabling CIS Benchmarks and AWS Foundational Security Best Practices)
  based on user-provided parameters.

Parameters:
  DisableCIS140Controls:
    Description: "Set to 'true' to disable CIS 1.4.0 controls, or 'false' to leave them enabled."
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - 'true'
      - 'false'
    Default: 'false'
  LogRetentionDays:
    Description: "The number of days to retain CloudWatch logs."
    Type: Number
    Default: 14
  PermissionsBoundary:
    Description: "ARN of the permissions boundary to attach to the IAM role. Leave empty if not required."
    Type: String
    Default: ''

Conditions:
  HasPermissionsBoundary: !Not [!Equals [!Ref PermissionsBoundary, ""]]

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "SecurityHubControlManager-Role-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns: []
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SecurityHubControlManagerPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - securityhub:*
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"
      PermissionsBoundary: !If 
        - HasPermissionsBoundary
        - !Ref PermissionsBoundary
        - !Ref "AWS::NoValue"

  SecurityHubControlManagerFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "SecurityHubControlManager-${AWS::StackName}"
      Description: "Lambda function to manage AWS Security Hub controls based on supplied parameters."
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          DISABLE_CIS_140_CONTROLS: !Ref DisableCIS140Controls
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import os
          import boto3

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Retrieve the parameter to determine if CIS 1.4.0 controls should be disabled.
              disable_control = os.environ.get('DISABLE_CIS_140_CONTROLS', 'false').lower() == 'true'
              
              securityhub = boto3.client('securityhub')
              
              # Example logic: Update Security Hub controls based on disable_control flag.
              # In production, iterate over controls or standards as required.
              try:
                  standards = securityhub.describe_standards()['Standards']
                  for standard in standards:
                      if disable_control and "CIS" in standard.get('Name', ''):
                          # Disable control example. Actual implementation may vary.
                          securityhub.update_standards_control(
                              StandardsControlArn=standard['StandardsControlArn'],
                              ControlStatus='DISABLED'
                          )
                      else:
                          securityhub.update_standards_control(
                              StandardsControlArn=standard['StandardsControlArn'],
                              ControlStatus='ENABLED'
                          )
                  return {
                      'statusCode': 200,
                      'body': json.dumps('Security Hub controls updated successfully.')
                  }
              except Exception as e:
                  return {
                      'statusCode': 500,
                      'body': json.dumps('Error updating Security Hub controls: ' + str(e))
                  }

  LambdaLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/lambda/${SecurityHubControlManagerFunction}"
      RetentionInDays: !Ref LogRetentionDays

Outputs:
  LambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Security Hub Control Manager Lambda function."
    Value: !GetAtt SecurityHubControlManagerFunction.Arn
  LambdaExecutionRoleARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Lambda execution IAM role."
    Value: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy a secure and compliant AWS Config setup with customizable retention policies,
  delivery configurations and audit logging. It provisions an S3 bucket (or integrates with an existing one),
  an SNS topic for notifications, an IAM role for AWS Config, a configuration recorder, a delivery channel,
  and a Lambda-backed custom resource to manage snapshot retention.

Parameters:
  ExistingS3BucketName:
    Type: String
    Default: ""
    Description: "Optional existing S3 bucket name to store AWS Config snapshots. Leave empty to create a new bucket."
  DeliveryFrequency:
    Type: String
    Default: TwentyFour_Hours
    AllowedValues:
      - One_Hour
      - TwentyFour_Hours
    Description: "Delivery frequency for AWS Config snapshots (One_Hour or TwentyFour_Hours)."
  RetentionDays:
    Type: Number
    Default: 2557
    Description: "Retention period (in days) for AWS Config snapshots."
  PermissionBoundaryArn:
    Type: String
    Default: ""
    Description: "Optional IAM Permission Boundary ARN to apply to the AWS Config role."

Conditions:
  CreateNewS3Bucket: !Equals [!Ref ExistingS3BucketName, ""]
  HasPermissionBoundary: !Not [!Equals [!Ref PermissionBoundaryArn, ""]]

Resources:

  ConfigS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Condition: CreateNewS3Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "aws-config-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::StackName}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigBucket"
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: "AWS Config compliance and audit snapshot storage"

  S3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Condition: CreateNewS3Bucket
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref ConfigS3Bucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: EnforceSSL
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:*"
            Resource:
              - !Sub "${ConfigS3Bucket.Arn}"
              - !Sub "${ConfigS3Bucket.Arn}/*"
            Condition:
              Bool:
                "aws:SecureTransport": "false"

  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigNotifications"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigNotifications"
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: "AWS Config notifications"

  AWSConfigRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-AWSConfigRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: config.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSConfigRole
      PermissionsBoundary: !If [HasPermissionBoundary, !Ref PermissionBoundaryArn, !Ref "AWS::NoValue"]
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-AWSConfigRole"
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: "Role for AWS Config service"

  ConfigRecorder:
    Type: AWS::Config::ConfigurationRecorder
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigRecorder"
      RoleARN: !GetAtt AWSConfigRole.Arn
      RecordingGroup:
        AllSupported: true
        IncludeGlobalResourceTypes: true

  ConfigDeliveryChannel:
    Type: AWS::Config::DeliveryChannel
    Properties:
      S3BucketName: !If
        - CreateNewS3Bucket
        - !Ref ConfigS3Bucket
        - !Ref ExistingS3BucketName
      SnsTopicARN: !Ref SNSTopic
      ConfigSnapshotDeliveryProperties:
        DeliveryFrequency: !Ref DeliveryFrequency

  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigLambdaRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ConfigSnapshotRetentionPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration
                  - s3:DeleteLifecycleConfiguration
                Resource: 
                  - !If 
                    - CreateNewS3Bucket
                    - !Sub "${ConfigS3Bucket.Arn}"
                    - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${ExistingS3BucketName}"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - config:*
                Resource: "*"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigLambdaRole"
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: "Execution role for custom Lambda managing AWS Config snapshot retention"

  ConfigRetentionLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ConfigRetentionLambda"
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Runtime: python3.14
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib3
          import boto3
          
          http = urllib3.PoolManager()
          
          def send(event, context, responseStatus, responseData, physicalResourceId=None, noEcho=False):
              responseUrl = event['ResponseURL']
              responseBody = {}
              responseBody['Status'] = responseStatus
              responseBody['Reason'] = 'See details in CloudWatch Log Stream: ' + context.log_stream_name
              responseBody['PhysicalResourceId'] = physicalResourceId or context.log_stream_name
              responseBody['StackId'] = event['StackId']
              responseBody['RequestId'] = event['RequestId']
              responseBody['LogicalResourceId'] = event['LogicalResourceId']
              responseBody['NoEcho'] = noEcho
              responseBody['Data'] = responseData
              json_responseBody = json.dumps(responseBody)
              headers = {'content-type': '', 'content-length': str(len(json_responseBody))}
              try:
                  http.request('PUT', responseUrl, body=json_responseBody, headers=headers)
              except Exception as e:
                  print("Failed to send response: " + str(e))
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              s3 = boto3.client('s3')
              retention_days = int(event['ResourceProperties']['RetentionDays'])
              bucket_name = event['ResourceProperties']['BucketName']
              try:
                  if event['RequestType'] in ['Create', 'Update']:
                      lifecycle_configuration = {
                          "Rules": [
                              {
                                  "ID": "AutoExpireAWSConfigSnapshots",
                                  "Status": "Enabled",
                                  "Prefix": "",
                                  "Expiration": {"Days": retention_days}
                              }
                          ]
                      }
                      s3.put_bucket_lifecycle_configuration(Bucket=bucket_name, LifecycleConfiguration=lifecycle_configuration)
                  elif event['RequestType'] == 'Delete':
                      try:
                          s3.delete_bucket_lifecycle(Bucket=bucket_name)
                      except Exception as e:
                          pass
                  send(event, context, "SUCCESS", {"Message": "Retention policy configured"})
              except Exception as e:
                  send(event, context, "FAILED", {"Message": str(e)})
                  raise

  ConfigRetentionCustomResource:
    Type: Custom::ConfigRetention
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt ConfigRetentionLambda.Arn
      RetentionDays: !Ref RetentionDays
      BucketName: !If
        - CreateNewS3Bucket
        - !Ref ConfigS3Bucket
        - !Ref ExistingS3BucketName

Outputs:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: "S3 bucket used for AWS Config snapshots"
    Value: !If
      - CreateNewS3Bucket
      - !Ref ConfigS3Bucket
      - !Ref ExistingS3BucketName
  SNSTopicARN:
    Description: "SNS Topic ARN for AWS Config notifications"
    Value: !Ref SNSTopic
  ConfigRecorderName:
    Description: "AWS Config Recorder Name"
    Value: !Ref ConfigRecorder
  ConfigRetentionLambdaFunctionName:
    Description: "Lambda Function Name for managing AWS Config snapshot retention"
    Value: !Ref ConfigRetentionLambda

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to manage an AWS account‐wide IAM password policy via a Lambda-backed custom resource.
  The template includes parameters for password complexity, expiration, reuse prevention, self-service password changes,
  a Lambda function that applies or removes the password policy using the AWS SDK, and an optional IAM permission boundary
  for the Lambda execution role.

Parameters:
  MinimumPasswordLength:
    Description: "Minimum number of characters for the password (default: 14)"
    Type: Number
    Default: 14

  RequireUppercase:
    Description: "Whether to require at least one uppercase letter (true or false)"
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
    Default: "true"

  RequireLowercase:
    Description: "Whether to require at least one lowercase letter (true or false)"
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
    Default: "true"

  RequireNumbers:
    Description: "Whether to require at least one number (true or false)"
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
    Default: "true"

  RequireSymbols:
    Description: "Whether to require at least one symbol (true or false)"
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
    Default: "true"

  PasswordExpiration:
    Description: "Number of days until a password expires (default: 90)"
    Type: Number
    Default: 90

  PasswordReusePrevention:
    Description: "Number of previous passwords to prevent re-use (default: 24)"
    Type: Number
    Default: 24

  EnableSelfServicePasswordChange:
    Description: "Whether IAM users can change their own password (true or false)"
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"
    Default: "true"

  LambdaPermissionBoundaryArn:
    Description: "Optional IAM permission boundary ARN to apply to the Lambda execution role (leave blank if not used)"
    Type: String
    Default: ""

Conditions:
  UseLambdaPermissionBoundary:
    Fn::Not:
      - Fn::Equals:
          - Ref: LambdaPermissionBoundaryArn
          - ""

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: UpdateAccountPasswordPolicyPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - iam:UpdateAccountPasswordPolicy
                  - iam:DeleteAccountPasswordPolicy
                Resource: "*"
      PermissionsBoundary:
        Fn::If:
          - UseLambdaPermissionBoundary
          - Ref: LambdaPermissionBoundaryArn
          - Ref: "AWS::NoValue"

  ApplyPasswordPolicyFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role:
        Fn::GetAtt:
          - LambdaExecutionRole
          - Arn
      Runtime: python3.14
      Timeout: 30
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import boto3
          import urllib3

          http = urllib3.PoolManager()

          def send_response(event, context, response_status, response_data, physical_resource_id):
              response_url = event['ResponseURL']
              response_body = {
                  'Status': response_status,
                  'Reason': 'See the details in CloudWatch Log Stream: ' + context.log_stream_name,
                  'PhysicalResourceId': physical_resource_id or context.log_stream_name,
                  'StackId': event['StackId'],
                  'RequestId': event['RequestId'],
                  'LogicalResourceId': event['LogicalResourceId'],
                  'Data': response_data
              }
              json_response_body = json.dumps(response_body)
              headers = {
                  'content-type' : '',
                  'content-length' : str(len(json_response_body))
              }
              response = http.request('PUT', response_url, body=json_response_body, headers=headers)
              return response

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              try:
                  request_type = event.get('RequestType')
                  if request_type == 'Delete':
                      # Remove the password policy
                      iam = boto3.client('iam')
                      iam.delete_account_password_policy()
                      response_data = { 'Message': 'Password policy removed' }
                      send_response(event, context, 'SUCCESS', response_data, 'CustomResourcePasswordPolicy')
                      return
                  else:
                      # Create or update the password policy
                      iam = boto3.client('iam')
                      params = {
                          'MinimumPasswordLength': int(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('MinimumPasswordLength', 14)),
                          'RequireSymbols': str(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('RequireSymbols', 'true')).lower() == 'true',
                          'RequireNumbers': str(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('RequireNumbers', 'true')).lower() == 'true',
                          'RequireUppercaseCharacters': str(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('RequireUppercase', 'true')).lower() == 'true',
                          'RequireLowercaseCharacters': str(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('RequireLowercase', 'true')).lower() == 'true',
                          'MaxPasswordAge': int(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('PasswordExpiration', 90)),
                          'PasswordReusePrevention': int(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('PasswordReusePrevention', 24)),
                          'AllowUsersToChangePassword': str(event.get('ResourceProperties', {}).get('EnableSelfServicePasswordChange', 'true')).lower() == 'true'
                      }
                      iam.update_account_password_policy(**params)
                      response_data = { 'Message': 'Password policy applied', 'Parameters': params }
                      send_response(event, context, 'SUCCESS', response_data, 'CustomResourcePasswordPolicy')
              except Exception as e:
                  send_response(event, context, 'FAILED', { 'Message': str(e) }, 'CustomResourcePasswordPolicy')
                  raise

      Tags:
        - Key: StackName
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Resource
          Value: LambdaFunction

  PasswordPolicyCustomResource:
    Type: Custom::AccountPasswordPolicy
    Properties:
      ServiceToken:
        Fn::GetAtt:
          - ApplyPasswordPolicyFunction
          - Arn
      MinimumPasswordLength: !Ref MinimumPasswordLength
      RequireUppercase: !Ref RequireUppercase
      RequireLowercase: !Ref RequireLowercase
      RequireNumbers: !Ref RequireNumbers
      RequireSymbols: !Ref RequireSymbols
      PasswordExpiration: !Ref PasswordExpiration
      PasswordReusePrevention: !Ref PasswordReusePrevention
      EnableSelfServicePasswordChange: !Ref EnableSelfServicePasswordChange

Outputs:
  LambdaFunctionArn:
    Description: "ARN of the Lambda function that manages the account password policy"
    Value:
      Fn::GetAtt:
        - ApplyPasswordPolicyFunction
        - Arn

  PasswordPolicyDetails:
    Description: "Parameters used to manage the IAM account password policy via the custom resource"
    Value: !Sub >
      {
        "MinimumPasswordLength": "${MinimumPasswordLength}",
        "RequireUppercase": "${RequireUppercase}",
        "RequireLowercase": "${RequireLowercase}",
        "RequireNumbers": "${RequireNumbers}",
        "RequireSymbols": "${RequireSymbols}",
        "MaxPasswordAge": "${PasswordExpiration}",
        "PasswordReusePrevention": "${PasswordReusePrevention}",
        "AllowUsersToChangePassword": "${EnableSelfServicePasswordChange}"
      }

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to provision a centralized alerting system using Amazon SNS.
  This template creates a primary SNS topic for aggregating notifications from AWS services,
  applies an SNS topic policy to allow authorized AWS services to publish alerts, and provisions
  configurable subscriptions (email and HTTP/HTTPS with retry policies). It also creates a fallback
  SNS topic and a CloudWatch Alarm that monitors failed notifications and triggers the fallback topic.

Parameters:
  EmailEndpoint:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Email address to subscribe to the primary SNS topic. Leave empty if not required.'
    Default: ""
  WebhookEndpoint:
    Type: String
    Description: 'HTTP/HTTPS endpoint (webhook URL) to subscribe to the primary SNS topic. Leave empty if not required.'
    Default: ""
  FallbackEmailEndpoint:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Fallback email address to subscribe to the fallback SNS topic. Leave empty if not required.'
    Default: ""

Conditions:
  HasEmailSubscription: !Not [!Equals [!Ref EmailEndpoint, ""]]
  HasWebhookSubscription: !Not [!Equals [!Ref WebhookEndpoint, ""]]
  HasFallbackEmailSubscription: !Not [!Equals [!Ref FallbackEmailEndpoint, ""]]

Resources:

  PrimarySNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "CentralAlertTopic-${AWS::Region}"

  PrimarySNSTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref PrimarySNSTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowAuthorizedServicesPublish
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - cloudwatch.amazonaws.com
                - rds.amazonaws.com
                - s3.amazonaws.com
                - budgets.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'SNS:Publish'
            Resource: !Ref PrimarySNSTopic

  EmailSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Condition: HasEmailSubscription
    Properties:
      Protocol: email
      Endpoint: !Ref EmailEndpoint
      TopicArn: !Ref PrimarySNSTopic

  WebhookSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Condition: HasWebhookSubscription
    Properties:
      Protocol: https
      Endpoint: !Ref WebhookEndpoint
      TopicArn: !Ref PrimarySNSTopic
      DeliveryPolicy: '{"http": {"defaultHealthyRetryPolicy": {"minDelayTarget": 20, "maxDelayTarget": 20, "numRetries": 3, "backoffFunction": "exponential"}}}'

  FallbackSNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "FallbackAlertTopic-${AWS::Region}"

  FallbackEmailSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Condition: HasFallbackEmailSubscription
    Properties:
      Protocol: email
      Endpoint: !Ref FallbackEmailEndpoint
      TopicArn: !Ref FallbackSNSTopic

  SNSFailureAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub "SNS-Failure-Alarm-${AWS::Region}"
      AlarmDescription: "Alarm for monitoring failed SNS notifications on the primary alert topic."
      Namespace: AWS/SNS
      MetricName: NumberOfNotificationsFailed
      Dimensions:
        - Name: TopicName
          Value: !GetAtt PrimarySNSTopic.TopicName
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 1
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref FallbackSNSTopic

Outputs:
  PrimarySNSTopicArn:
    Description: "ARN of the primary SNS topic for alerts."
    Value: !Ref PrimarySNSTopic
  FallbackSNSTopicArn:
    Description: "ARN of the fallback SNS topic for alert redundancy."
    Value: !Ref FallbackSNSTopic

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: AWS Batch environment for executing containerized batch jobs with S3 input/output, VPC, and associated resources.

Resources:

  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  Subnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-Subnet"

  BatchSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Security group for AWS Batch compute environment"
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BatchSecurityGroup"

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "batch-data-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a self-contained web server environment.
  It provisions a VPC, public subnet, internet gateway, route table, security group,
  IAM role and instance profile for S3 access, and an EC2 instance running Apache and PHP.
Parameters:
  S3Bucket:
    Description: "Name of the S3 bucket containing the ZIP file with web content"
    Type: String
    Default: "my-s3-bucket"
  S3Key:
    Description: "S3 key (path) to the ZIP file with web content"
    Type: String
    Default: "content.zip"
  KeyNameParameter:
    Description: "Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance"
    Type: String
Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: "WebServerVPC"

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: "PublicSubnet"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: "WebServerIGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: "PublicRouteTable"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  WebServerSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Allow HTTP and SSH access"
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: "WebServerSG"

  EC2InstanceIAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: "S3ReadAccessPolicy"
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${S3Bucket}"
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${S3Bucket}/*"

  EC2InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: "/"
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceIAMRole

  WebServerInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t2.micro
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref WebServerSecurityGroup
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfile
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs}}"
      KeyName: !Ref KeyNameParameter
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub |
          #!/bin/bash
          yum update -y
          yum install -y httpd php unzip aws-cli
          aws s3 cp s3://${S3Bucket}/${S3Key} /tmp/content.zip
          unzip /tmp/content.zip -d /var/www/html
          systemctl enable httpd
          systemctl start httpd
    Metadata:
      AWS::CloudFormation::Init:
        config:
          packages:
            yum:
              httpd: []
              php: []
              unzip: []
          services:
            sysvinit:
              httpd:
                enabled: true
                ensureRunning: true

Outputs:
  InstancePublicIP:
    Description: "Public IP address of the web server instance"
    Value: !GetAtt WebServerInstance.PublicIp
  WebURL:
    Description: "URL to access the web server"
    Value: !Join
      - ""
      - - "http://"
        - !GetAtt WebServerInstance.PublicDnsName

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template for a basic AWS Lambda function using Node.js.
  The function logs incoming event details and returns a value extracted from the event.
  An IAM Role with permissions for CloudWatch Logs is attached to the function.

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaLoggingPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"

  LambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: BasicLambdaFunction
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: nodejs22.x
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          exports.handler = async (event) => {
              console.log("Received event:", event);
              // Return a value extracted from the input event; replace 'value' with the correct key as needed.
              return event && event.value ? event.value : null;
          };

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFront distribution fronting a regional API Gateway REST API with a mock backend returning a static "hello world" JSON response.
Resources:
  ApiGatewayRestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: HelloWorldApi
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - REGIONAL

  ApiGatewayResourceHello:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      ParentId: !GetAtt ApiGatewayRestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: hello

  ApiGatewayMethodHelloGet:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref ApiGatewayResourceHello
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: MOCK
        RequestTemplates:
          application/json: '{"statusCode": 200}'
        IntegrationResponses:
          - StatusCode: 200
            ResponseTemplates:
              application/json: '{"message": "hello world"}'
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200
          ResponseModels:
            application/json: Empty

  ApiGatewayDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn: ApiGatewayMethodHelloGet
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      StageName: production

  CloudFrontDistribution:
    Type: AWS::CloudFront::Distribution
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
        Enabled: true
        Origins:
          - Id: ApiGatewayOrigin
            DomainName: !Sub "${ApiGatewayRestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com"
            OriginPath: "/production"
            CustomOriginConfig:
              OriginProtocolPolicy: https-only
        DefaultCacheBehavior:
          TargetOriginId: ApiGatewayOrigin
          ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
          AllowedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
          CachedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
          ForwardedValues:
            QueryString: true
            Cookies:
              Forward: none
          MinTTL: 0
          DefaultTTL: 0
          MaxTTL: 0

Outputs:
  CloudFrontDomainName:
    Description: "CloudFront Distribution Domain Name"
    Value: !GetAtt CloudFrontDistribution.DomainName

  ApiGatewayEndpoint:
    Description: "API Gateway endpoint URL"
    Value: !Sub "https://${ApiGatewayRestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/production/hello"
