AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to create a developer IAM role with configurable permission boundaries.
  The role grants broad IAM privileges for creating and modifying IAM resources while enforcing governance controls,
  including mandatory resource naming conventions ("MyTeam*"), mandatory Owner tags, and permission boundary requirements.
Parameters:
  StrictBoundaryEnforcement:
    Type: String
    Description: "Toggle strict permission boundary enforcement (Enabled/Disabled). When Enabled, developers must pass the default permission boundary."
    AllowedValues:
      - Enabled
      - Disabled
    Default: Enabled
  TeamOwner:
    Type: String
    Description: "The team-specific Owner tag value that must be applied to all developer-managed IAM resources."
    Default: MyTeamOwner
Conditions:
  IsStrictEnforcement:
    Fn::Equals:
      - !Ref StrictBoundaryEnforcement
      - "Enabled"
Resources:
  DefaultPermissionBoundaryPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy
    Properties:
      ManagedPolicyName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-DefaultBoundaryPolicy"
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - s3:*
              - iam:Get*
              - iam:List*
            Resource: "*"
  DeveloperIAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-DeveloperRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action: "sts:AssumeRole"
      PermissionsBoundary: !If
        - IsStrictEnforcement
        - !Ref DefaultPermissionBoundaryPolicy
        - !Ref "AWS::NoValue"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: DeveloperIAMPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Deny
                Action:
                  - iam:CreateRole
                  - iam:UpdateRole
                  - iam:TagRole
                  - iam:CreatePolicy
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringNotEquals:
                    aws:RequestTag/Owner: !Ref TeamOwner
              - Effect: Deny
                Action:
                  - iam:CreateRole
                  - iam:UpdateRole
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringNotLike:
                    iam:RoleName: "MyTeam*"
              - Effect: Deny
                Action:
                  - iam:CreatePolicy
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringNotLike:
                    iam:PolicyName: "MyTeam*"
              - Effect: Deny
                Action:
                  - iam:CreateRole
                  - iam:UpdateRole
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringNotEquals:
                    iam:PermissionsBoundary: !Ref DefaultPermissionBoundaryPolicy
Outputs:
  DeveloperIAMRoleArn:
    Description: "ARN of the developer IAM role"
    Value: !GetAtt DeveloperIAMRole.Arn
  DefaultPermissionBoundaryPolicyArn:
    Description: "ARN of the default permission boundary policy"
    Value: !Ref DefaultPermissionBoundaryPolicy

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template for an AppSync GraphQL API (PetShopAPI) backed by a Lambda function providing a mock pet inventory query service.

Resources:
  PetLambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "PetLambdaExecutionRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaBasicExecutionPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"

  PetLambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "PetInventoryFunction-${AWS::StackName}"
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt PetLambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Runtime: python3.12
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          
          def handler(event, context):
              # Return a static list of pet records
              pets = [
                  {"id": "1", "name": "Fido", "type": "Dog"},
                  {"id": "2", "name": "Whiskers", "type": "Cat"},
                  {"id": "3", "name": "Tweety", "type": "Bird"}
              ]
              return pets

  AppSyncGraphQLApi:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::GraphQLApi
    Properties:
      Name: PetShopAPI
      AuthenticationType: API_KEY
      AdditionalAuthenticationProviders: []

  AppSyncApiKey:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::ApiKey
    Properties:
      ApiId: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.ApiId

  AppSyncGraphQLSchema:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::GraphQLSchema
    Properties:
      ApiId: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.ApiId
      Definition: |
        schema {
          query: Query
        }
        
        type Query {
          getPets: [Pet]
        }
        
        type Pet {
          id: ID
          name: String
          type: String
        }

  AppSyncLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "AppSyncLambdaInvocationRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: appsync.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: AppSyncLambdaInvokePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt PetLambdaFunction.Arn

  PetLambdaDataSource:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::DataSource
    Properties:
      ApiId: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.ApiId
      Name: PetLambdaDataSource
      Type: AWS_LAMBDA
      LambdaConfig:
        LambdaFunctionArn: !GetAtt PetLambdaFunction.Arn
      ServiceRoleArn: !GetAtt AppSyncLambdaRole.Arn

  GetPetsResolver:
    Type: AWS::AppSync::Resolver
    DependsOn:
      - PetLambdaDataSource
    Properties:
      ApiId: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.ApiId
      TypeName: Query
      FieldName: getPets
      DataSourceName: PetLambdaDataSource
      RequestMappingTemplate: |
        {
          "version" : "2017-02-28",
          "operation" : "Invoke",
          "payload" : {}
        }
      ResponseMappingTemplate: |
        $util.toJson($ctx.result)
        
Outputs:
  GraphQLApiId:
    Description: "AppSync GraphQL API Id"
    Value: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.ApiId
  GraphQLApiUrl:
    Description: "AppSync GraphQL API URL"
    Value: !GetAtt AppSyncGraphQLApi.GraphQLUrl
  GraphQLApiKey:

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  AWS CloudFormation template to deploy a web application environment with integrated S3, DynamoDB,
  and EC2 resources in a VPC with public subnet access. The EC2 instance is configured with Apache/PHP,
  preloaded lab files, and bootstraps application setup including AWS SDK installation and dynamic configuration.
  
Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance.
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Default: ReplaceWithYourKeyPair

Mappings:
  AWSRegionToAMI:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c94855ba95c71c99
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-0b2f6494ff0b07a0e
    eu-west-1:
      AMI: ami-047bb4163c506cd98

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: WebApp-VPC

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: WebApp-IGW

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicRouteTable

  DefaultPublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow HTTP and SSH access
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-WebApp-SG

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3FullAccess
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonDynamoDBFullAccess
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EC2-WebApp-Role

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: "/"
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  WebServerInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t2.micro
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      ImageId: !FindInMap
        - AWSRegionToAMI
        - !Ref "AWS::Region"
        - AMI
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: 0
          SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
          GroupSet:
            - !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: !Sub
          - |
            #!/bin/bash
            exec > /var/log/user-data.log 2>&1
            yum update -y
            # Install Apache, PHP, and necessary packages
            yum install -y httpd php php-mbstring php-xml php-json
            service httpd start
            chkconfig httpd on
            # Install AWS SDK for PHP via Composer
            yum install -y php-cli composer
            cd /var/www/html
            composer require aws/aws-sdk-php
            # Preload lab files for the web application
            echo "<?php phpinfo(); ?>" > index.php
            # Dynamic configuration using region and S3 bucket name
            REGION='${AWS::Region}'
            BUCKET_NAME='${StaticAssetsBucket}'
            CONFIG_FILE="/var/www/html/config.php"
            echo "<?php" > $CONFIG_FILE
            echo "define('AWS_REGION', '${AWS::Region}');" >> $CONFIG_FILE
            echo "define('ASSETS_BUCKET', '${StaticAssetsBucket}');" >> $CONFIG_FILE
            echo "?>" >> $CONFIG_FILE
            # Insert DynamoDB data ingestion logic (example using AWS CLI and JSON files)
            if [ -d "/var/www/html/data" ]; then
              for file in /var/www/html/data/*.json; do
                aws dynamodb batch-write-item --request-items file://$file --region ${AWS::Region}
              done
            fi
            # Upload static assets to S3 bucket (example)
            if [ -d "/var/www/html/assets" ]; then
              aws s3 sync /var/www/html/assets s3://${StaticAssetsBucket}/assets --region ${AWS::Region}
            fi
          - {StaticAssetsBucket: !Ref StaticAssetsBucket}

  StaticAssetsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "static-assets-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}"
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  DynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: AWS-Services
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: PartitionKey
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: SortKey
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: PartitionKey
          KeyType: HASH
        - AttributeName: SortKey
          KeyType: RANGE
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

Outputs:
  InstancePublicIP:
    Description: Public IP of the web server EC2 instance.
    Value: !GetAtt WebServerInstance.PublicIp
  StaticAssetsBucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket for static assets.
    Value: !Ref StaticAssetsBucket
  DynamoDBTableName:
    Description: Name of the DynamoDB table.
    Value: !Ref DynamoDBTable

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template that deploys a Lambda function to perform dynamic arithmetic operations
  (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) based on user-provided parameters with a conditional
  failure mechanism. A custom resource (Custom::helper) is used to invoke the Lambda function during stack
  create/update events, with the computed result propagated to CloudFormation outputs.

Parameters:
  FirstNumber:
    Description: "The first number in the arithmetic operation."
    Type: Number
    Default: 0
  SecondNumber:
    Description: "The second number in the arithmetic operation."
    Type: Number
    Default: 0
  Operator:
    Description: "Arithmetic operator to use."
    Type: String
    AllowedValues:
      - add
      - subtract
      - multiply
      - divide
    Default: add
  SimulateFailure:
    Description: "Set to 'true' to simulate a failure in the custom resource."
    Type: String
    Default: "false"
    AllowedValues:
      - "true"
      - "false"

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: /
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaBasicExecution
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: arn:aws:logs:*:*:*

  ArithmeticFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.12
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib.request
          import urllib.error

          def send_response(event, context, response_status, response_data, physical_resource_id=None, noecho=False, reason=None):
              response_url = event['ResponseURL']
              response_body = {
                  'Status': response_status,
                  'Reason': reason or ("See the details in CloudWatch Log Stream: " + context.log_stream_name),
                  'PhysicalResourceId': physical_resource_id or context.log_stream_name,
                  'StackId': event['StackId'],
                  'RequestId': event['RequestId'],
                  'LogicalResourceId': event['LogicalResourceId'],
                  'NoEcho': noecho,
                  'Data': response_data
              }
              json_response_body = json.dumps(response_body)
              headers = {
                  'content-type': '',
                  'content-length': str(len(json_response_body))
              }
              try:
                  req = urllib.request.Request(url=response_url, data=bytearray(json_response_body, 'utf-8'), headers=headers, method='PUT')
                  with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as response:
                      print("Status code:", response.getcode())
              except Exception as e:
                  print("send_response failed:", e)

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              print("Received event: " + json.dumps(event))
              try:
                  # Extract parameters from event ResourceProperties
                  props = event.get('ResourceProperties', {})
                  first = float(props.get('FirstNumber', 0))
                  second = float(props.get('SecondNumber', 0))
                  operator = props.get('Operator', 'add').lower()
                  simulate_failure = props.get('SimulateFailure', 'false').lower() == 'true'

                  # If simulating failure, send a failure response.
                  if simulate_failure:
                      response = {"Result": "Simulated failure triggered."}
                      send_response(event, context, "FAILED", response, reason="Simulated failure as requested")
                      return

                  result = None
                  if operator == "add":
                      result = first + second
                  elif operator == "subtract":
                      result = first - second
                  elif operator == "multiply":
                      result = first * second
                  elif operator == "divide":
                      if second == 0:
                          raise Exception("Division by zero is not allowed.")
                      result = first / second
                  else:
                      raise Exception("Unsupported operator provided.")

                  response = {"Result": str(result)}
                  send_response(event, context, "SUCCESS", response)
              except Exception as e:
                  print("Exception: " + str(e))
                  response = {"Result": "Error: " + str(e)}
                  send_response(event, context, "FAILED", response, reason=str(e))

  CustomHelper:
    Type: Custom::helper
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt ArithmeticFunction.Arn
      FirstNumber: !Ref FirstNumber
      SecondNumber: !Ref SecondNumber
      Operator: !Ref Operator
      SimulateFailure: !Ref SimulateFailure

Outputs:
  CalculationResult:
    Description: "Result of the arithmetic operation performed by the custom resource."
    Value: !GetAtt CustomHelper.Result

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template for a serverless calculator workflow.
  This template deploys a Lambda function ("Calculator") that performs arithmetic operations,
  a CloudWatch Logs group for Lambda log retention (3 days), and an AWS Step Functions state machine
  ("CalculatorStateMachine") that routes requests based on the "operator" input.

Resources:

  CalculatorLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: CalculatorLambdaRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

  Calculator:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: Calculator
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt CalculatorLambdaRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import math

          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              operator = event.get('operator')
              operands = event.get('operands', [])
              
              if not operator or not isinstance(operands, list) or len(operands) < 1:
                  return {
                      "error": "Invalid input. Expecting 'operator' and a non-empty list of 'operands'."
                  }
              
              try:
                  if operator == "add":
                      result = sum(operands)
                  elif operator == "subtract":
                      result = operands[0] - sum(operands[1:]) if len(operands) > 1 else operands[0]
                  elif operator == "multiply":
                      result = 1
                      for num in operands:
                          result *= num
                  elif operator == "divide":
                      result = operands[0]
                      for num in operands[1:]:
                          if num == 0:
                              return {"error": "Division by zero."}
                          result /= num
                  else:
                      return {"error": "Unsupported operator."}
                  return {"result": result}
              except Exception as e:
                  return {"error": str(e)}
  
  CalculatorLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: /aws/lambda/Calculator
      RetentionInDays: 3

  CalculatorStateMachineRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: CalculatorStateMachineRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: states.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CalculatorStateMachinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt Calculator.Arn

  CalculatorStateMachine:
    Type: AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine
    Properties:
      StateMachineName: CalculatorStateMachine
      RoleArn: !GetAtt CalculatorStateMachineRole.Arn
      DefinitionString: !Sub >
        {
          "Comment": "State machine for a calculator workflow that invokes a Lambda function based on operator.",
          "StartAt": "ChoiceState",
          "States": {
            "ChoiceState": {
              "Type": "Choice",
              "Choices": [
                {
                  "Variable": "$.operator",
                  "StringEquals": "add",
                  "Next": "AddOperation"
                },
                {
                  "Variable": "$.operator",
                  "StringEquals": "subtract",
                  "Next": "SubtractOperation"
                },
                {
                  "Variable": "$.operator",
                  "StringEquals": "multiply",
                  "Next": "MultiplyOperation"
                },
                {
                  "Variable": "$.operator",
                  "StringEquals": "divide",
                  "Next": "DivideOperation"
                }
              ],
              "Default": "UnsupportedOperator"
            },
            "AddOperation": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "${Calculator.Arn}",
              "End": true
            },
            "SubtractOperation": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "${Calculator.Arn}",
              "End": true
            },
            "MultiplyOperation": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "${Calculator.Arn}",
              "End": true
            },
            "DivideOperation": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "${Calculator.Arn}",
              "End": true
            },
            "UnsupportedOperator": {
              "Type": "Fail",
              "Error": "UnsupportedOperator",
              "Cause": "The provided operator is not supported."
            }
          }
        }

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  CloudFormation template for an automated image recognition pipeline.
  It creates an S3 bucket for image uploads, an SNS topic (with SMS subscription),
  a Step Functions state machine that validates image extensions, invokes Amazon Rekognition,
  and sends SMS alerts when human faces are detected. It also provisions the necessary
  IAM roles and a custom Lambda function used to empty the S3 bucket during stack deletion.

Resources:

  ImagesBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-images"
    DeletionPolicy: Delete

  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-sns-topic"

  SNSTopicSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      TopicArn: !Ref SNSTopic
      Protocol: sms
      Endpoint: "+14071113333"

  EventBridgeSfnRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EB-SFN-Role"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeStepFunctions
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: states:StartExecution
                Resource: !Ref StateMachine

  StepFunctionsExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SFN-ExecutionRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: states.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: StepFunctionsRekognitionS3Access
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource: !Sub "${ImagesBucket.Arn}/*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - rekognition:DetectLabels
                Resource: "*"

  EvaluateLabelsLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EvaluateLabelsLambdaRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SNSPublishPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sns:Publish
                Resource: !Ref SNSTopic

  EvaluateLabelsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-evaluate-labels"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt EvaluateLabelsLambdaRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import os
          import boto3

          def handler(event, context):
              sns_topic_arn = os.environ.get('SNS_TOPIC_ARN')
              client = boto3.client('sns')
              labels = event.get('Labels', [])
              human_detected = False
              for label in labels:
                  name = label.get('Name', '').lower()
                  if name in ['human', 'person']:
                      human_detected = True
                      break
              if human_detected:
                  message = "Human face detected in s3://{}/{}".format(event.get('Bucket', ''), event.get('Key', ''))
                  client.publish(TopicArn=sns_topic_arn, Message=message)
              return event
      Environment:
        Variables:
          SNS_TOPIC_ARN: !Ref SNSTopic

  BucketEmptyLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-BucketEmptyLambdaRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: S3EmptyPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:ListBucket
                Resource: !GetAtt ImagesBucket.Arn
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:DeleteObject
                Resource: !Sub "${ImagesBucket.Arn}/*"

  BucketEmptyFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-bucket-empty"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt BucketEmptyLambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib3
          import boto3
          import traceback

          http = urllib3.PoolManager()

          def send_response(event, context, responseStatus, responseData, physicalResourceId=None, noEcho=False):
              responseUrl = event['ResponseURL']
              responseBody = {
                  'Status': responseStatus,
                  'Reason': 'See the details in CloudWatch Log Stream: ' + context.log_stream_name,
                  'PhysicalResourceId': physicalResourceId or context.log_stream_name,
                  'StackId': event['StackId'],
                  'RequestId': event['RequestId'],
                  'LogicalResourceId': event['LogicalResourceId'],
                  'NoEcho': noEcho,
                  'Data': responseData
              }
              json_responseBody = json.dumps(responseBody)
              headers = {
                  'content-type': '',
                  'content-length': str(len(json_responseBody))
              }
              try:
                  response = http.request('PUT', responseUrl, body=json_responseBody, headers=headers)
              except Exception as e:
                  print("send_response failed: " + str(e))

          def handler(event, context):
              try:
                  request_type = event.get('RequestType')
                  bucket_name = event['ResourceProperties']['BucketName']
                  s3 = boto3.resource('s3')
                  bucket = s3.Bucket(bucket_name)
                  if request_type == 'Delete':
                      bucket.objects.all().delete()
                  # For Create and Update, no bucket emptying is needed
                  send_response(event, context, "SUCCESS", {})
              except Exception as e:
                  print("Exception: " + str(e))
                  traceback_str = traceback.format_exc()
                  send_response(event, context, "FAILED", {"Message": str(e), "Trace": traceback_str})
  
  BucketEmptyCustomResource:
    Type: Custom::EmptyBucket
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt BucketEmptyFunction.Arn
      BucketName: !Ref ImagesBucket

  StateMachine:
    Type: AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine
    Properties:
      StateMachineName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-state-machine"
      RoleArn: !GetAtt StepFunctionsExecutionRole.Arn
      DefinitionString: !Sub |
        {
          "Comment": "Automated image recognition pipeline",
          "StartAt": "ValidateExtension",
          "States": {
            "ValidateExtension": {
              "Type": "Choice",
              "Choices": [
                {
                  "Or": [
                    { "Variable": "$.detail.object.key", "StringMatches": "*.jpg" },
                    { "Variable": "$.detail.object.key", "StringMatches": "*.jpeg" },
                    { "Variable": "$.detail.object.key", "StringMatches": "*.png" }
                  ],
                  "Next": "DetectLabels"
                }
              ],
              "Default": "NotAnImage"
            },
            "NotAnImage": {
              "Type": "Succeed"
            },
            "DetectLabels": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::aws-sdk:rekognition:detectLabels",
              "Parameters": {
                "Image": {
                  "S3Object": {
                    "Bucket.$": "$.detail.bucket.name",
                    "Name.$": "$.detail.object.key"
                  }
                },
                "MinConfidence": 80
              },
              "ResultPath": "$.RekognitionResult",
              "Next": "EvaluateLabels"
            },
            "EvaluateLabels": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "${EvaluateLabelsFunction}",
                "Payload": {
                  "Labels.$": "$.RekognitionResult.Labels",
                  "Bucket.$": "$.detail.bucket.name",
                  "Key.$": "$.detail.object.key"
                }
              },
              "End": true
            }
          }
        }

  S3ImageUploadEventRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-s3-image-upload"
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - "aws.s3"
        detail-type:
          - "Object Created"
        resources:
          - !GetAtt ImagesBucket.Arn
      Targets:
        - Id: "StepFunctionTarget"
          Arn: !GetAtt StateMachine.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeSfnRole.Arn
          InputTransformer:
            InputPathsMap:
              bucket: "$.detail.requestParameters.bucketName"
              objectKey: "$.detail.requestParameters.key"
              detail: "$.detail"
            InputTemplate: >
              {
                "detail": {
                  "bucket": {"name": <bucket>},
                  "object": {"key": <objectKey>}
                }
              }

Outputs:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: "Name of the S3 bucket for image uploads"
    Value: !Ref ImagesBucket
  StateMachineARN:
    Description: "ARN of the Step Functions state machine"
    Value: !GetAtt StateMachine.Arn
  SNSTopicARN:
    Description: "ARN of the SNS topic for SMS alerts"
    Value: !Ref SNSTopic

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  CloudFormation template to deploy EC2 instances managed by AWS Systems Manager (SSM)
  using Auto Scaling Groups with Launch Templates for Linux (Amazon Linux 2) and Windows.
  It includes a VPC with a public subnet, Internet Gateway, Route Table, Security Group with HTTP access,
  and an IAM Role and Instance Profile (SSMRole) with AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore policy attached.

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  PublicSubnet:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicRouteTable"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Allow inbound HTTP access and outbound traffic for SSM operations"
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-InstanceSG"

  SSMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: SSMRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: SSMRole

  SSMInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      InstanceProfileName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SSMInstanceProfile"
      Roles:
        - !Ref SSMRole

  LinuxLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LinuxLaunchTemplate"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs}}"
        InstanceType: t3.micro
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Name: !Ref SSMInstanceProfile
        UserData: !Base64 |
          #!/bin/bash
          yum update -y
          # Install and start the SSM Agent
          yum install -y amazon-ssm-agent
          systemctl enable amazon-ssm-agent
          systemctl start amazon-ssm-agent

  WindowsLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-WindowsLaunchTemplate"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-windows-latest/Windows_Server-2019-English-Full-Base}}"
        InstanceType: t3.small
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Name: !Ref SSMInstanceProfile

  LinuxAutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref LinuxLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt LinuxLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: "1"
      MaxSize: "3"
      DesiredCapacity: "1"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: LinuxSSMInstance
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

  WindowsAutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref WindowsLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt WindowsLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: "1"
      MaxSize: "3"
      DesiredCapacity: "1"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: WindowsSSMInstance
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Multi-tier AWS network environment with secure public/private subnets,
  NAT Gateway, SSM-managed EC2 instances, and IAM roles for Systems Manager.

Parameters:
  VpcCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC.

Resources:
  VPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCidr
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  # Public Subnets
  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.1.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet1"

  PublicSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.2.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet2"

  # Private Subnets
  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.11.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet1"

  PrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      CidrBlock: 10.0.12.0/24
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateSubnet2"

  # Elastic IP for NAT Gateway
  NatEIP:
    Type: AWS::EC2::EIP
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      Domain: vpc

  # NAT Gateway in Public Subnet1
  NatGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NatGateway
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      AllocationId: !GetAtt NatEIP.AllocationId
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-NATGateway"

  # Public Route Table with route to Internet Gateway
  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicRT"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnet1RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  PublicSubnet2RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  # Private Route Table with route to NAT Gateway
  PrivateRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateRT"

  PrivateRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: 0.0.0.0/0
      NatGatewayId: !Ref NatGateway

  PrivateSubnet1RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  PrivateSubnet2RouteTableAssociation:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PrivateRouteTable

  # IAM Role for EC2 with SSM Managed Instance Core policy
  EC2SSMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Path: /
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-EC2SSMRole"

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: /
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2SSMRole

  # Security Group for Public Instance: allow inbound HTTP from anywhere
  PublicInstanceSG:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow inbound HTTP from anywhere
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicInstanceSG"

  # Security Group for Private Instance: allow inbound HTTPS from PublicInstanceSG only
  PrivateInstanceSG:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow inbound HTTPS from Public Instance Security Group only
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref PublicInstanceSG
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateInstanceSG"

  # EC2 Instance in Public Subnet hosting Apache/PHP (PublicInstance)
  PublicInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs}}"
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref PublicInstanceSG
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: |
          #!/bin/bash
          yum update -y
          yum install -y httpd php
          systemctl start httpd
          systemctl enable httpd
          echo "<?php phpinfo(); ?>" > /var/www/html/index.php
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicInstance"

  # EC2 Instance in Private Subnet managed via SSM (PrivateInstance)
  PrivateInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.micro
      SubnetId: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
      ImageId: "{{resolve:ssm:/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-ebs}}"
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref InstanceProfile
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref PrivateInstanceSG
      UserData:
        Fn::Base64: |
          #!/bin/bash
          # Private instance setup. Managed by SSM.
          yum update -y
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PrivateInstance"

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: VPC ID
    Value: !Ref VPC
  PublicSubnet1:
    Description: Public Subnet 1 ID
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet1
  PublicSubnet2:
    Description: Public Subnet 2 ID
    Value: !Ref PublicSubnet2
  PrivateSubnet1:
    Description: Private Subnet 1 ID
    Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet1
  PrivateSubnet2:
    Description: Private Subnet 2 ID
    Value: !Ref PrivateSubnet2
  PublicInstanceId:
    Description: Public EC2 Instance ID
    Value: !Ref PublicInstance
  PrivateInstanceId:
    Description: Private EC2 Instance ID
    Value: !Ref PrivateInstance
  NATGatewayId:
    Description: NAT Gateway ID
    Value: !Ref NatGateway
  InstanceProfile:
    Description: IAM Instance Profile for EC2 instances managed by SSM
    Value: !Ref InstanceProfile

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Automated image analysis pipeline using S3, Lambda, Step Functions, Rekognition, and SNS.
  The pipeline detects human faces in uploaded images and sends SMS alerts.

Parameters:
  AlertPhoneNumber:
    Type: String
    Default: "+1234567890"
    Description: The phone number (in E.164 format, e.g. +1234567890) to receive SMS alerts.

Resources:
  ########################################
  # S3 Bucket: Image Analysis Bucket with inline NotificationConfiguration
  ########################################
  ImageAnalysisBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "image-analysis-bucket-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
      NotificationConfiguration:
        LambdaConfigurations:
          - Event: "s3:ObjectCreated:*"
            Function: !GetAtt ImageUploadHandler.Arn

  ########################################
  # IAM Role for Primary Lambda (ImageUploadHandler)
  ########################################
  PrimaryLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "PrimaryLambdaRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: PrimaryLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - states:StartExecution
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:states:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:stateMachine:ImageAnalysisStateMachine-${AWS::StackName}"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"
  
  ########################################
  # IAM Role for Rekognition Analysis Lambda
  ########################################
  AnalysisLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "RekognitionLambdaRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: AnalysisLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - rekognition:DetectFaces
                  - rekognition:DetectLabels
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::image-analysis-bucket-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}/*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"
  
  ########################################
  # IAM Role for SMS Notification Lambda
  ########################################
  NotificationLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "NotificationLambdaRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: NotificationLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sns:Publish
                Resource: !Ref SNSTopic
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "*"
  
  ########################################
  # IAM Role for Step Functions State Machine
  ########################################
  StepFunctionsRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "StepFunctionsRole-${AWS::StackName}"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: states.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: StepFunctionsInvokeLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource:
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:RekognitionHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
                  - !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:SmsNotificationHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                  - logs:DescribeLogStreams
                  - logs:DescribeLogGroups
                Resource: "*"
  
  ########################################
  # Lambda Function: Image Upload Handler
  # This Lambda is triggered by S3 and starts the Step Functions state machine.
  ########################################
  ImageUploadHandler:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "ImageUploadHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt PrimaryLambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 30
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json, boto3, os
          sf_client = boto3.client('stepfunctions')
          STATE_MACHINE_ARN = os.environ.get('STATE_MACHINE_ARN')
          def handler(event, context):
              try:
                  record = event['Records'][0]
                  bucket = record['s3']['bucket']['name']
                  key = record['s3']['object']['key']
                  input_data = {
                      "bucket": bucket,
                      "key": key
                  }
                  response = sf_client.start_execution(
                      stateMachineArn=STATE_MACHINE_ARN,
                      input=json.dumps(input_data)
                  )
                  return response
              except Exception as e:
                  print("Error starting execution:", e)
                  raise e
      Environment:
        Variables:
          STATE_MACHINE_ARN: !Sub "arn:aws:states:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:stateMachine:ImageAnalysisStateMachine-${AWS::StackName}"
  
  ########################################
  # Lambda Permission for S3 to invoke ImageUploadHandler
  ########################################
  LambdaPermissionForS3:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    DependsOn:
      - ImageUploadHandler
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "ImageUploadHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: s3.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::image-analysis-bucket-${AWS::AccountId}-${AWS::Region}"
  
  ########################################
  # Lambda Function: Rekognition Analysis Handler
  # This Lambda analyzes the image using Rekognition to detect human faces.
  ########################################
  AnalysisHandler:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "RekognitionHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt AnalysisLambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 60
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json, boto3
          rekognition = boto3.client('rekognition')
          def handler(event, context):
              bucket = event.get('bucket')
              key = event.get('key')
              response = rekognition.detect_faces(
                  Image={'S3Object': {'Bucket': bucket, 'Name': key}},
                  Attributes=['ALL']
              )
              faces = response.get('FaceDetails', [])
              humanDetected = True if faces else False
              return {"humanDetected": humanDetected, "bucket": bucket, "key": key}
  
  ########################################
  # Lambda Function: SMS Notification Handler
  # This Lambda sends an SMS alert via SNS when human faces are detected.
  ########################################
  SmsNotificationHandler:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "SmsNotificationHandler-${AWS::StackName}"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt NotificationLambdaRole.Arn
      Timeout: 30
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json, boto3, os
          sns = boto3.client('sns')
          PHONE_NUMBER = os.environ.get('ALERT_PHONE_NUMBER')
          def handler(event, context):
              message = "Alert: Human face(s) detected in the image."
              response = sns.publish(
                  PhoneNumber=PHONE_NUMBER,
                  Message=message
              )
              return {"status": "SMS sent", "response": response}
      Environment:
        Variables:
          ALERT_PHONE_NUMBER: !Ref AlertPhoneNumber
  
  ########################################
  # CloudWatch Log Group for Step Functions Logging
  ########################################
  StateMachineLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/vendedlogs/stepfunctions/ImageAnalysisStateMachine-${AWS::StackName}"
      RetentionInDays: 14
  
  ########################################
  # Step Functions State Machine Definition
  ########################################
  ImageAnalysisStateMachine:
    Type: AWS::StepFunctions::StateMachine
    Properties:
      StateMachineName: !Sub "ImageAnalysisStateMachine-${AWS::StackName}"
      RoleArn: !GetAtt StepFunctionsRole.Arn
      DefinitionString: !Sub |
        {
          "Comment": "State machine for image analysis workflow",
          "StartAt": "AnalyzeImage",
          "States": {
            "AnalyzeImage": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:RekognitionHandler-${AWS::StackName}",
                "Payload": {
                  "bucket.$": "$.bucket",
                  "key.$": "$.key"
                }
              },
              "ResultPath": "$.analysisResult",
              "Next": "CheckForHuman"
            },
            "CheckForHuman": {
              "Type": "Choice",
              "Choices": [
                {
                  "Variable": "$.analysisResult.Payload.humanDetected",
                  "BooleanEquals": true,
                  "Next": "SendSMSNotification"
                }
              ],
              "Default": "Done"
            },
            "SendSMSNotification": {
              "Type": "Task",
              "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
              "Parameters": {
                "FunctionName": "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:SmsNotificationHandler-${AWS::StackName}",
                "Payload": {
                  "bucket.$": "$.bucket",
                  "key.$": "$.key"
                }
              },
              "ResultPath": "$.notificationResult",
              "Next": "Done"
            },
            "Done": {
              "Type": "Succeed"
            }
          }
        }
      LoggingConfiguration:
        Level: ALL
        IncludeExecutionData: true
        Destinations:
          - CloudWatchLogsLogGroup:
              LogGroupArn: !GetAtt StateMachineLogGroup.Arn
  
  ########################################
  # SNS Topic for SMS Alerts
  ########################################
  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub "HumanDetectionAlerts-${AWS::StackName}"
  
  ########################################
  # CloudWatch Logs Resource Policy for Step Functions
  ########################################
  StepFunctionsLogResourcePolicy:
    Type: AWS::Logs::ResourcePolicy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: !Sub "StepFunctionsLogPolicy-${AWS::StackName}"
      PolicyDocument: !Sub |
        {
          "Version": "2012-10-17",
          "Statement": [
            {
              "Sid": "AllowStepFunctionsToWriteLogs",
              "Effect": "Allow",
              "Principal": { "AWS": "${StepFunctionsRoleArn}" },
              "Action": [
                "logs:CreateLogStream",
                "logs:PutLogEvents"
              ],
              "Resource": "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/vendedlogs/stepfunctions/ImageAnalysisStateMachine-${AWS::StackName}:*"
            }
          ]
        }
      # Use a two-argument !Sub to substitute the StepFunctionsRole ARN.
      StepFunctionsRoleArn: !GetAtt StepFunctionsRole.Arn

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket for image uploads.
    Value: !Ref ImageAnalysisBucket
  StateMachineArn:
    Description: ARN of the Step Functions state machine.
    Value: !GetAtt ImageAnalysisStateMachine.Arn
  SNSTopicArn:
    Description: ARN of the SNS Topic for SMS alerts.
    Value: !Ref SNSTopic

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  Serverless slot machine web application with automated content deployment.
  This template creates an S3 bucket (static website hosting), a Lambda function to simulate slot spins,
  a custom Lambda (backed custom resource) to deploy website assets, and a Cognito Identity Pool with an IAM
  role for unauthenticated users authorized only to invoke the slot machine Lambda.

Resources:

  SlotMachineBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-slotmachine-bucket"
      WebsiteConfiguration:
        IndexDocument: index.html
        ErrorDocument: error.html
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: false
        BlockPublicPolicy: false
        IgnorePublicAcls: false
        RestrictPublicBuckets: false

  SlotMachineBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref SlotMachineBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: PublicReadForWebsite
            Effect: Allow
            Principal: "*"
            Action: "s3:GetObject"
            Resource: !Sub "${SlotMachineBucket.Arn}/*"

  SlotMachineLambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SlotMachineLambdaRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: [lambda.amazonaws.com]
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SlotMachineLambdaLoggingPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*"

  SlotMachineLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SlotMachineFunction"
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt SlotMachineLambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: 10
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import random
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Define slot symbols
              symbols = ["Cherry", "Lemon", "Orange", "Bell", "Seven"]
              # Simulate three reels
              result = [random.choice(symbols) for _ in range(3)]
              # Determine win: simple logic - win if all three symbols match
              win = (result[0] == result[1] == result[2])
              return {
                  "result": result,
                  "win": win
              }

  CognitoIdentityPool:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::IdentityPool
    Properties:
      IdentityPoolName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IdentityPool"
      AllowUnauthenticatedIdentities: true

  CognitoUnauthRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-CognitoUnauthRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Federated: cognito-identity.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                "cognito-identity.amazonaws.com:aud": !Ref CognitoIdentityPool
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CognitoInvokeLambdaPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:InvokeFunction
                Resource: !GetAtt SlotMachineLambda.Arn

  CustomResourceLambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-CRLambdaRole"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: [lambda.amazonaws.com]
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CRLambdaLoggingAndS3Policy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                Resource: !Sub "${SlotMachineBucket.Arn}/*"

  CustomResourceLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-WebsiteDeployFunction"
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: python3.14
      Role: !GetAtt CustomResourceLambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: 300
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import urllib.request
          import boto3
          def handler(event, context):
              s3 = boto3.client('s3')
              request_type = event.get('RequestType')
              bucket = event['ResourceProperties']['BucketName']
              website_zip_url = event['ResourceProperties']['WebsiteZipUrl']
              identity_pool_id = event['ResourceProperties']['CognitoIdentityPoolId']
              region = event['ResourceProperties']['Region']
              
              # For simplicity, we are simulating a deployment that injects the Cognito and region config
              # by uploading a configuration file to the S3 website bucket.
              config = {
                  "CognitoIdentityPoolId": identity_pool_id,
                  "Region": region
              }
              s3.put_object(
                  Bucket=bucket,
                  Key='config.json',
                  Body=json.dumps(config),
                  ContentType='application/json'
              )
              
              # In a full implementation, you might download the ZIP from website_zip_url,
              # unzip, inject configurations into the HTML/JS assets, and upload them to S3.
              
              # Send a response back to CloudFormation
              import cfnresponse
              response_data = {'Message': 'Website deployment completed.'}
              cfnresponse.send(event, context, cfnresponse.SUCCESS, response_data)

  WebsiteDeploymentCustomResource:
    Type: Custom::WebsiteDeployment
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt CustomResourceLambda.Arn
      BucketName: !Ref SlotMachineBucket
      WebsiteZipUrl: "https://example.com/prebuilt.zip"
      CognitoIdentityPoolId: !Ref CognitoIdentityPool
      Region: !Ref "AWS::Region"

Outputs:
  WebsiteURL:
    Description: "URL for the static website hosting slot machine assets"
    Value: !GetAtt SlotMachineBucket.WebsiteURL
  CognitoIdentityPoolId:
    Description: "Cognito Identity Pool ID for anonymous access"
    Value: !Ref CognitoIdentityPool

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: >
  Jenkins Server Deployment using Auto Scaling across two public subnets in a multi-AZ VPC.
  This template creates a VPC, two public subnets, an Internet Gateway, Route Table, Network ACL,
  Security Group, IAM Role with SSM permissions, a Launch Template with a UserData script for Jenkins installation,
  and an Auto Scaling Group that ensures high availability.

Mappings:
  RegionMap:
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-0c94855ba95c71c99
    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-07d0cf3af28718ef8
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0bdb828fd58c52235
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-00a208c7cdba991ea

Resources:

  JenkinsVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: "10.0.0.0/16"
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-VPC"

  InternetGateway:
    Type: AWS::EC2::InternetGateway
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-IGW"

  VPCGatewayAttachment:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      InternetGatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicRouteTable:
    Type: AWS::EC2::RouteTable
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicRT"

  PublicRoute:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Route
    DependsOn: VPCGatewayAttachment
    Properties:
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable
      DestinationCidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      GatewayId: !Ref InternetGateway

  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      CidrBlock: "10.0.1.0/24"
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet1"

  PublicSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      CidrBlock: "10.0.2.0/24"
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs ""]
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-PublicSubnet2"

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  SubnetRouteTableAssociation2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetRouteTableAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      RouteTableId: !Ref PublicRouteTable

  JenkinsNetworkAcl:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAcl
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-NetworkAcl"

  NaclEntryInboundHTTP:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 100
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      PortRange:
        From: 80
        To: 80

  NaclEntryInboundHTTPS:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 110
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      PortRange:
        From: 443
        To: 443

  NaclEntryInboundJenkins:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 120
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      PortRange:
        From: 8080
        To: 8080

  NaclEntryInboundSSH:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 130
      Protocol: 6
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: false
      CidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"
      PortRange:
        From: 22
        To: 22

  NaclEntryOutboundAll:
    Type: AWS::EC2::NetworkAclEntry
    Properties:
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl
      RuleNumber: 100
      Protocol: -1
      RuleAction: allow
      Egress: true
      CidrBlock: "0.0.0.0/0"

  NaclAssociationSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetNetworkAclAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet1
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl

  NaclAssociationSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SubnetNetworkAclAssociation
    Properties:
      SubnetId: !Ref PublicSubnet2
      NetworkAclId: !Ref JenkinsNetworkAcl

  JenkinsInstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "Allow SSH, HTTP, HTTPS, and Jenkins port access"
      VpcId: !Ref JenkinsVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 8080
          ToPort: 8080
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: "0.0.0.0/0"
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SG"

  JenkinsSSMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Path: "/"
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: JenkinsSSMAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:UpdateInstanceInformation
                  - ssm:DescribeInstanceProperties
                  - ssmmessages:CreateControlChannel
                  - ssmmessages:CreateDataChannel
                  - ssmmessages:OpenControlChannel
                  - ssmmessages:OpenDataChannel
                  - ec2messages:SendReply
                  - ec2messages:GetMessages
                Resource: "*"
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  JenkinsInstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Path: "/"
      Roles:
        - !Ref JenkinsSSMRole

  JenkinsLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-LaunchTemplate"
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !FindInMap [RegionMap, !Ref "AWS::Region", AMI]
        InstanceType: t3.medium
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref JenkinsInstanceSecurityGroup
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt JenkinsInstanceProfile.Arn
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: |
            #!/bin/bash
            exec > /var/log/user-data.log 2>&1
            yum update -y
            amazon-linux-extras install java-openjdk11 -y
            wget -O /etc/yum.repos.d/jenkins.repo https://pkg.jenkins.io/redhat-stable/jenkins.repo
            rpm --import https://pkg.jenkins.io/redhat-stable/jenkins.io.key
            yum install jenkins -y
            systemctl enable jenkins
            systemctl start jenkins

  JenkinsAutoScalingGroup:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      AutoScalingGroupName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-ASG"
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref JenkinsLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt JenkinsLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      MinSize: "1"
      MaxSize: "3"
      DesiredCapacity: "1"
      HealthCheckType: EC2
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: JenkinsServer
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: "VPC Id"
    Value: !Ref JenkinsVPC
  AutoScalingGroupName:
    Description: "Auto Scaling Group Name"
    Value: !Ref JenkinsAutoScalingGroup
  LaunchTemplateId:
    Description: "Launch Template Id"
    Value: !Ref JenkinsLaunchTemplate
